target_include_directories(custom_master PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}"
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/src"
)
target_compile_options(custom_master PRIVATE ${VSIM_COMPILE_FLAGS})
target_compile_definitions(custom_master PRIVATE ${VSIM_COMPILE_DEFS})
add_dependencies(custom_master deps generate_headers)

# ##############################################################################
# Per-CPU targets
# ##############################################################################

# Wires a per-CPU executable up with the include paths, compile/link options,
# verilated CPU library, and aceasim glue. Relies on per-CPU variables set
# inside the foreach loop below (CPU_NAME, CPU_UPPER, RTL, OBJ_SC_DIR,
# _aceasim_lib, _stub_aceasim_lib).
macro(vsim_add_cpu_executable _target _source)
  add_executable(${_target} ${ARGN} ${_source})
  target_include_directories(${_target} SYSTEM PRIVATE
    ${VSIM_INCLUDE_DIRS}
    "${OBJ_SC_DIR}"
  )
  target_include_directories(${_target} PRIVATE
    "${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}"
    "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/src"
    "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/hybrid/include"
  )
  target_compile_options(${_target} PRIVATE ${VSIM_COMPILE_FLAGS})
  target_compile_definitions(${_target} PRIVATE
    ${VSIM_COMPILE_DEFS}
    VSIM_CPU_${CPU_UPPER}
  )
  target_link_options(${_target} PRIVATE ${VSIM_LINK_FLAGS})
  target_link_libraries(${_target} PRIVATE
    custom_master
    "${OBJ_SC_DIR}/V${RTL}__ALL.a"
    ${VSIM_DEP_LIBS}
    "${OBJ_SC_DIR}/libverilated.a"
    ${VSIM_LINK_LIBS}
  )
  if(_aceasim_lib)
    target_link_libraries(${_target} PRIVATE
      -Wl,--whole-archive
      "${_aceasim_lib}"
      -Wl,--no-whole-archive
    )
  endif()
  if(_stub_aceasim_lib)
    target_link_options(${_target} PRIVATE
      "-Wl,--undefined=get_libaceasim_version")
    target_link_libraries(${_target} PRIVATE "${_stub_aceasim_lib}")
  endif()
  add_dependencies(${_target} deps generate_headers)
  if(NOT VSIM_NO_VERILATOR)
    add_dependencies(${_target} verilated_${CPU_NAME})
  endif()
endmacro()

foreach(CPU_NAME IN LISTS VSIM_CPU_LIST)
  string(TOUPPER "${CPU_NAME}" CPU_UPPER)
  string(TOLOWER "${CPU_NAME}" CPU_LOWER)

  set(RTL "${VSIM_CPU_${CPU_UPPER}_RTL_PACKAGE}")
  set(LIBACEASIM_PATH "${VSIM_CPU_${CPU_UPPER}_LIBACEASIM_PATH}")
  set(OBJ_SC_DIR "${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/V${CPU_NAME}/obj_sc")

  # Resolve libaceasim

  # _aceasim_lib: user's libaceasim.a generated from COPILOT.
  # _stub_aceasim_lib: stubs-built libaceasim.a, always linked to sim_<cpu> when
  # given a string.
  # The stubs provide all required four DPI-C symbols (ace, ace_pre_dec, vace, vace_dec)
  # as defaults in case _aceasim_lib doesn't include any of them. This can happen
  # when ACE file only has ACE-Scalar or ACE-RVV instructions, so that COPILOT
  # doesn't generate the other types of DPI-C modules.
  #
  if(LIBACEASIM_PATH AND NOT LIBACEASIM_PATH MATCHES "^stubs")
    set(_aceasim_lib "${LIBACEASIM_PATH}")
  endif()

content.text: Important finding: `src/main.cpp` unconditionally includes `hybrid/state_abi.h` (line 4) and uses `hybrid_state_v1` (line 434). Hybrid is baked in, not optional. So `HYBRID_DRIVER_DIR` must be **required** for vsim to build, defaulting to `${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/..`. I'll adjust the plan accordingly.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# End-to-end roundtrip:
#   1. QEMU 1 + plugin: drains state at `csrwi 0x7C0, 0`.
#   2. vsim: resumes, runs body, drains at `csrwi 0x7C0, 1`, exits 200.
#   3. vsim's --qemu-binary path: spawns QEMU 2 with -gdb -S -semihosting,
#      restores GPRs+PC via gdbstub, continues.
#   4. QEMU 2 hits the RISC-V semihosting ebreak-triple, exits with the
#      semihosting exit_code (low 8 bits of a2 in the existing fixture).
#
# Fixture-parameterized:
#   FIXTURE     name of .elf under hybrid/test/  (default: handoff_roundtrip)
#   EXPECT_RC   expected vsim process exit code  (default: 3)
#
# Required environment:
#   VSIM_BIN         path to sim_<cpu> binary (host-runnable)
#   VSIM_QEMU        path to qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN      path to libhybrid_handoff.so
#   VSIM_ELF         path to <fixture>.elf  (overrides FIXTURE if set)

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-handoff_roundtrip}"
EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-3}"

VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/${FIXTURE}.elf}"

for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built; run 'make' under hybrid/test and hybrid/qemu_plugin"
    exit 77   # CTest skip code when SKIP_RETURN_CODE is set
  fi
done

STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT

echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3] QEMU 1: drain entry state -> $STATE_QEMU"
"$VSIM_QEMU" -M virt -bios none -nographic \
  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_QEMU" >/dev/null 2>&1

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 2/3] vsim: resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2"
set +e
timeout 60 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" != "$EXPECT_RC" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: expected exit $EXPECT_RC, got $RC"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 3/3] PASS: round-trip exit code $RC"

# Build tiny RV64 fixtures used by hybrid smoke tests.
# Default toolchain matches /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/...

TOOLCHAIN ?= /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d
CC        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-gcc
LD        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-ld
OBJDUMP   := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump

CFLAGS  := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
LDFLAGS := -T spin.ld -nostdlib -static

# Tier-1 round-trip e2e fixtures share handoff_roundtrip.ld.
RT_FIXTURES := rt_all_gprs rt_abi_aliases rt_sign_bits rt_pc_jump \
               rt_long_roi rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh \
               rt_shared_mem rt_csr_survival

.PHONY: all clean dump

all: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf handoff_roundtrip.elf \
     $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

spin.elf: spin.S spin.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c spin.S -o spin.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) spin.o -o $@

handoff.elf: handoff.S handoff.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff.S -o handoff.o
	$(LD) -T handoff.ld -nostdlib -static handoff.o -o $@

handoff_exit.elf: handoff_exit.S handoff_exit.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_exit.S -o handoff_exit.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_exit.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_exit.o -o $@

handoff_roundtrip.elf: handoff_roundtrip.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_roundtrip.S -o handoff_roundtrip.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_roundtrip.o -o $@

$(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static $*.o -o $@

dump: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d spin.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff_exit.elf

clean:
	rm -f spin.o spin.elf handoff.o handoff.elf handoff_exit.o handoff_exit.elf \
	      handoff_roundtrip.o handoff_roundtrip.elf \
	      $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.o) $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

# Tier-2 round-trip e2e: cross-simulator memory survival via shared mmap.
#
# QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2 all map the same backing file. Phase A writes a
# pattern in QEMU 1; Phase B verifies + mutates in vsim; Phase C re-reads
# in QEMU 2. Memory must survive both handoffs.
#
# Required environment:
#   VSIM_BIN     path to sim_<cpu> binary (host-runnable)
#   VSIM_QEMU    path to qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  path to libhybrid_handoff.so

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

FIXTURE=rt_shared_mem
EXPECT_RC=0

VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/${FIXTURE}.elf}"

for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built; run 'make' under hybrid/test and hybrid/qemu_plugin"
    exit 77
  fi
done

# 128 MB matches the QEMU virt machine default; vsim must use the same size.
SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-rt-shared.XXXXXX)
STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM" "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT

truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"

echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3] QEMU 1 (shared mmap): drain entry state"
"$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
  -machine virt,memory-backend=mem \
  -bios none -nographic \
  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_QEMU" >/dev/null 2>&1

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 2/3] vsim (shared mmap): resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2"
set +e
timeout 60 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --shared-mem-path "$SHARED_MEM" \
  --shared-mem-size "$SHARED_SIZE" \
  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" != "$EXPECT_RC" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: expected exit $EXPECT_RC, got $RC"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 3/3] PASS: round-trip exit code $RC"

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Negative test: a corrupted --resume-from-qemu state file must trigger a
3	# fail-fast in vsim with a recognizable error message, not a segfault, hang,
4	# or silent forward run on garbage state.
5	
6	set -euo pipefail
7	
8	SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
9	REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)
10	
11	VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
12	VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/handoff_roundtrip.elf}"
13	
14	for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
15	  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
16	    echo "skip: $f not built"
17	    exit 77
18	  fi
19	done
20	
21	BAD_STATE=$(mktemp)
22	SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
23	trap 'rm -f "$BAD_STATE" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT
24	
25	# 32 bytes of zeros: shorter than hybrid_state_v1, hits the short-read branch.
26	dd if=/dev/zero of="$BAD_STATE" bs=32 count=1 status=none
27	
28	set +e
29	timeout 10 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
30	  --resume-from-qemu "$BAD_STATE" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
31	RC=$?
32	set -e
33	
34	# 0    - silent pass on garbage (BAD)
35	# 124  - bash timeout fired -> vsim hung (BAD)
36	# 200  - hybrid handoff exit (BAD: drain ran, state was treated as valid)
37	# else - any non-zero error path (OK)
38	if [ "$RC" -eq 0 ] || [ "$RC" -eq 200 ] || [ "$RC" -eq 124 ]; then
39	  echo "FAIL[rt_corrupt_state]: expected non-zero, non-200, non-timeout exit; got $RC"
40	  echo "--- sim log ---"
41	  cat "$SIM_LOG"
42	  exit 1
43	fi
44	
45	if ! grep -Eq "short read|bad magic|cannot open" "$SIM_LOG"; then
46	  echo "FAIL[rt_corrupt_state]: stderr did not match 'short read|bad magic|cannot open'"
47	  echo "--- sim log ---"
48	  cat "$SIM_LOG"
49	  exit 1
50	fi
51	
52	echo "[rt_corrupt_state] PASS: vsim fail-fast with rc=$RC"
53

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Negative test: a corrupted --resume-from-qemu state file must trigger a
# fail-fast in vsim with a recognizable error message, not a segfault, hang,
# or silent forward run on garbage state.

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/handoff_roundtrip.elf}"

for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built"
    exit 77
  fi
done

BAD_STATE=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$BAD_STATE" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT

# 32 bytes of zeros: shorter than hybrid_state_v1, hits the short-read branch.
dd if=/dev/zero of="$BAD_STATE" bs=32 count=1 status=none

set +e
timeout 10 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$BAD_STATE" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

# 0    - silent pass on garbage (BAD)
# 124  - bash timeout fired -> vsim hung (BAD)
# 200  - hybrid handoff exit (BAD: drain ran, state was treated as valid)
# else - any non-zero error path (OK)
if [ "$RC" -eq 0 ] || [ "$RC" -eq 200 ] || [ "$RC" -eq 124 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_corrupt_state]: expected non-zero, non-200, non-timeout exit; got $RC"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

if ! grep -Eq "short read|bad magic|cannot open" "$SIM_LOG"; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_corrupt_state]: stderr did not match 'short read|bad magic|cannot open'"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[rt_corrupt_state] PASS: vsim fail-fast with rc=$RC"

51	  echo "FAIL[rt_gdbstub_timeout]: expected non-zero non-200 exit, got $RC"

53	  exit 1
54	fi

56	if [ "$RC" -eq 124 ]; then
57	  echo "FAIL[rt_gdbstub_timeout]: bash 30 s timeout fired -> vsim hung"
58	  cat "$SIM_LOG"
59	  exit 1
60	fi

62	if [ "$ELAPSED" -gt 25 ]; then
63	  echo "FAIL[rt_gdbstub_timeout]: elapsed=${ELAPSED}s (>25s suggests hang)"
64	  cat "$SIM_LOG"
65	  exit 1
66	fi
67	
68	echo "[rt_gdbstub_timeout] PASS: vsim exit=$RC elapsed=${ELAPSED}s"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Negative test: --qemu-binary opens the gdbstub port but never responds to
# packets. Vsim's gdbstub Session must time out on the read side and exit
# non-zero promptly, not block forever waiting for an ack that never comes.

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)
STUB="$SCRIPT_DIR/qemu_silent_stub.py"

VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/handoff_roundtrip.elf}"

for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF" "$STUB"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built"
    exit 77
  fi
done

STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT

# Real QEMU 1 produces a valid entry-state snapshot so vsim reaches the
# exit-csrwi drain and tries to spawn the (silent) handback peer.
"$VSIM_QEMU" -M virt -bios none -nographic \
  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_QEMU" >/dev/null 2>&1

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_gdbstub_timeout]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

START=$(date +%s)
set +e
timeout 30 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --qemu-binary "$STUB" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e
ELAPSED=$(($(date +%s) - START))

if [ "$RC" -eq 0 ] || [ "$RC" -eq 200 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_gdbstub_timeout]: expected non-zero non-200 exit, got $RC"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

if [ "$RC" -eq 124 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_gdbstub_timeout]: bash 30 s timeout fired -> vsim hung"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

if [ "$ELAPSED" -gt 25 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_gdbstub_timeout]: elapsed=${ELAPSED}s (>25s suggests hang)"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[rt_gdbstub_timeout] PASS: vsim exit=$RC elapsed=${ELAPSED}s"

29	# Phase A: real QEMU 1 produces a valid entry-state snapshot so vsim has
30	# something to resume from and will reach the exit-csrwi drain.
31	"$VSIM_QEMU" -M virt -bios none -nographic \
32	  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
33	  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_QEMU" >/dev/null 2>&1

36	  echo "FAIL[rt_no_qemu_binary]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
37	  exit 1
38	fi
39	
40	START=$(date +%s)
41	set +e
42	timeout 30 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
43	  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
44	  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
45	  --qemu-binary /nonexistent/qemu-system-riscv64 >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
46	RC=$?
47	set -e
48	ELAPSED=$(($(date +%s) - START))
49	
50	if [ "$RC" -ne 127 ]; then
51	  echo "FAIL[rt_no_qemu_binary]: expected exit 127 (exec failure), got $RC"
52	  echo "elapsed=${ELAPSED}s"

60	  exit 1
61	fi
62	
63	# No zombie left: any leftover child of this shell would show up here.
64	if jobs -r | grep -q .; then
65	  echo "FAIL[rt_no_qemu_binary]: background jobs still running"
66	  exit 1
67	fi
68	
69	echo "[rt_no_qemu_binary] PASS: vsim exit=$RC elapsed=${ELAPSED}s"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Negative test: --qemu-binary points at a missing file. Vsim's drain must
# still fire and the spawn-fail must propagate as exit 127 (the standard
# exec-failure code), not as a 5 s connect_loopback timeout that masquerades
# as something else, and not as a hang that ties up CTest.

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/handoff_roundtrip.elf}"

for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built"
    exit 77
  fi
done

STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT

# Phase A: real QEMU 1 produces a valid entry-state snapshot so vsim has
# something to resume from and will reach the exit-csrwi drain.
"$VSIM_QEMU" -M virt -bios none -nographic \
  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_QEMU" >/dev/null 2>&1

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_no_qemu_binary]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

START=$(date +%s)
set +e
timeout 30 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --qemu-binary /nonexistent/qemu-system-riscv64 >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e
ELAPSED=$(($(date +%s) - START))

if [ "$RC" -ne 127 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_no_qemu_binary]: expected exit 127 (exec failure), got $RC"
  echo "elapsed=${ELAPSED}s"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

if [ "$ELAPSED" -gt 15 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_no_qemu_binary]: vsim took ${ELAPSED}s (>15s suggests a hang)"
  exit 1
fi

# No zombie left: any leftover child of this shell would show up here.
if jobs -r | grep -q .; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_no_qemu_binary]: background jobs still running"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[rt_no_qemu_binary] PASS: vsim exit=$RC elapsed=${ELAPSED}s"

I'll default it to `${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/..` (parent workspace) with a fatal-error gate on missing headers.

Now executing the file operations. Parent-side imports first.

5	#   3. vsim's --qemu-binary path: spawns QEMU 2 with -gdb -S -semihosting,
6	#      restores GPRs+PC via gdbstub, continues.
7	#   4. QEMU 2 hits the RISC-V semihosting ebreak-triple, exits with the
8	#      semihosting exit_code (low 8 bits of a2 in the existing fixture).
9	#

12	#   EXPECT_RC   expected vsim process exit code  (default: 3)
13	#

16	#   VSIM_QEMU        path to qemu-system-riscv64
17	#   VSIM_PLUGIN      path to libhybrid_handoff.so
18	#   VSIM_ELF         path to <fixture>.elf  (overrides FIXTURE if set)
19

21

24

26	EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-3}"
27	
28	VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
29	VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
30	VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
31	VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/${FIXTURE}.elf}"
32	
33	for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
34	  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
35	    echo "skip: $f not built; run 'make' under hybrid/test and hybrid/qemu_plugin"
36	    exit 77   # CTest skip code when SKIP_RETURN_CODE is set
37	  fi
38	done
39	
40	STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
41	STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
42	SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
43	trap 'rm -f "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT
44

46	"$VSIM_QEMU" -M virt -bios none -nographic \

51	  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
52	  exit 1
53	fi

55	echo "[$FIXTURE 2/3] vsim: resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2"
56	set +e
57	timeout 60 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
58	  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
59	  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
60	  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
61	RC=$?
62	set -e
63	
64	if [ "$RC" != "$EXPECT_RC" ]; then
65	  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: expected exit $EXPECT_RC, got $RC"
66	  echo "--- sim log ---"
67	  cat "$SIM_LOG"
68	  exit 1
69	fi
70	
71	echo "[$FIXTURE 3/3] PASS: round-trip exit code $RC"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# End-to-end roundtrip:
#   1. QEMU 1 + plugin: drains state at `csrwi 0x7C0, 0`.
#   2. vsim: resumes, runs body, drains at `csrwi 0x7C0, 1`, exits 200.
#   3. vsim's --qemu-binary path: spawns QEMU 2 with -gdb -S -semihosting,
#      restores GPRs+PC via gdbstub, continues.
#   4. QEMU 2 hits the RISC-V semihosting ebreak-triple, exits with the
#      semihosting exit_code (low 8 bits of a2 in the existing fixture).
#
# Fixture-parameterized:
#   FIXTURE     name of .elf under hybrid/test/  (default: handoff_roundtrip)
#   EXPECT_RC   expected vsim process exit code  (default: 3)

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# End-to-end roundtrip:
#   1. QEMU 1 + plugin: drains state at `csrwi 0x7C0, 0`.
#   2. vsim: resumes, runs body, drains at `csrwi 0x7C0, 1`, exits 200.
#   3. vsim's --qemu-binary path: spawns QEMU 2 with -gdb -S -semihosting,
#      restores GPRs+PC via gdbstub, continues.
#   4. QEMU 2 hits the RISC-V semihosting ebreak-triple, exits with the
#      semihosting exit_code (low 8 bits of a2 in the existing fixture).
#
# Fixture-parameterized:
#   FIXTURE     name of .elf under hybrid/test/  (default: handoff_roundtrip)
#   EXPECT_RC   expected vsim process exit code  (default: 3)
#
# Required environment:
#   VSIM_BIN         path to sim_<cpu> binary (host-runnable)
#   VSIM_QEMU        path to qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN      path to libhybrid_handoff.so
#   VSIM_ELF         path to <fixture>.elf  (overrides FIXTURE if set)

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-handoff_roundtrip}"
EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-3}"

VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/${FIXTURE}.elf}"

for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built; run 'make' under hybrid/test and hybrid/qemu_plugin"
    exit 77   # CTest skip code when SKIP_RETURN_CODE is set
  fi
done

STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT

echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3] QEMU 1: drain entry state -> $STATE_QEMU"
"$VSIM_QEMU" -M virt -bios none -nographic \
  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_QEMU" >/dev/null 2>&1

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 2/3] vsim: resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2"
set +e
timeout 60 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" != "$EXPECT_RC" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: expected exit $EXPECT_RC, got $RC"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 3/3] PASS: round-trip exit code $RC"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Negative test: a corrupted --resume-from-qemu state file must trigger a
# fail-fast in vsim with a recognizable error message, not a segfault, hang,
# or silent forward run on garbage state.

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/handoff_roundtrip.elf}"

for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built"
    exit 77
  fi
done

BAD_STATE=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$BAD_STATE" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT

# 32 bytes of zeros: shorter than hybrid_state_v1, hits the short-read branch.
dd if=/dev/zero of="$BAD_STATE" bs=32 count=1 status=none

set +e
timeout 10 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$BAD_STATE" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

# 0    - silent pass on garbage (BAD)
# 124  - bash timeout fired -> vsim hung (BAD)
# 200  - hybrid handoff exit (BAD: drain ran, state was treated as valid)
# else - any non-zero error path (OK)
if [ "$RC" -eq 0 ] || [ "$RC" -eq 200 ] || [ "$RC" -eq 124 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_corrupt_state]: expected non-zero, non-200, non-timeout exit; got $RC"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

if ! grep -Eq "short read|bad magic|cannot open" "$SIM_LOG"; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_corrupt_state]: stderr did not match 'short read|bad magic|cannot open'"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[rt_corrupt_state] PASS: vsim fail-fast with rc=$RC"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Negative test: --qemu-binary opens the gdbstub port but never responds to
# packets. Vsim's gdbstub Session must time out on the read side and exit
# non-zero promptly, not block forever waiting for an ack that never comes.

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)
STUB="$SCRIPT_DIR/qemu_silent_stub.py"

VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/handoff_roundtrip.elf}"

for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF" "$STUB"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built"
    exit 77
  fi
done

STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT

# Real QEMU 1 produces a valid entry-state snapshot so vsim reaches the
# exit-csrwi drain and tries to spawn the (silent) handback peer.
"$VSIM_QEMU" -M virt -bios none -nographic \
  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_QEMU" >/dev/null 2>&1

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_gdbstub_timeout]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

START=$(date +%s)
set +e
timeout 30 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --qemu-binary "$STUB" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e
ELAPSED=$(($(date +%s) - START))

if [ "$RC" -eq 0 ] || [ "$RC" -eq 200 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_gdbstub_timeout]: expected non-zero non-200 exit, got $RC"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

if [ "$RC" -eq 124 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_gdbstub_timeout]: bash 30 s timeout fired -> vsim hung"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

if [ "$ELAPSED" -gt 25 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_gdbstub_timeout]: elapsed=${ELAPSED}s (>25s suggests hang)"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[rt_gdbstub_timeout] PASS: vsim exit=$RC elapsed=${ELAPSED}s"

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Negative test: --qemu-binary points at a missing file. Vsim's drain must
3	# still fire and the spawn-fail must propagate as exit 127 (the standard
4	# exec-failure code), not as a 5 s connect_loopback timeout that masquerades
5	# as something else, and not as a hang that ties up CTest.
6

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Negative test: --qemu-binary points at a missing file. Vsim's drain must
# still fire and the spawn-fail must propagate as exit 127 (the standard
# exec-failure code), not as a 5 s connect_loopback timeout that masquerades
# as something else, and not as a hang that ties up CTest.

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/handoff_roundtrip.elf}"

for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built"
    exit 77
  fi
done

STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT

# Phase A: real QEMU 1 produces a valid entry-state snapshot so vsim has
# something to resume from and will reach the exit-csrwi drain.
"$VSIM_QEMU" -M virt -bios none -nographic \
  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_QEMU" >/dev/null 2>&1

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_no_qemu_binary]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

START=$(date +%s)
set +e
timeout 30 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --qemu-binary /nonexistent/qemu-system-riscv64 >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e
ELAPSED=$(($(date +%s) - START))

if [ "$RC" -ne 127 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_no_qemu_binary]: expected exit 127 (exec failure), got $RC"
  echo "elapsed=${ELAPSED}s"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

if [ "$ELAPSED" -gt 15 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_no_qemu_binary]: vsim took ${ELAPSED}s (>15s suggests a hang)"
  exit 1
fi

# No zombie left: any leftover child of this shell would show up here.
if jobs -r | grep -q .; then
  echo "FAIL[rt_no_qemu_binary]: background jobs still running"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[rt_no_qemu_binary] PASS: vsim exit=$RC elapsed=${ELAPSED}s"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Tier-2 round-trip e2e: cross-simulator memory survival via shared mmap.
#
# QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2 all map the same backing file. Phase A writes a
# pattern in QEMU 1; Phase B verifies + mutates in vsim; Phase C re-reads
# in QEMU 2. Memory must survive both handoffs.
#
# Required environment:
#   VSIM_BIN     path to sim_<cpu> binary (host-runnable)
#   VSIM_QEMU    path to qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  path to libhybrid_handoff.so

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

FIXTURE=rt_shared_mem
EXPECT_RC=0

VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/${FIXTURE}.elf}"

for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built; run 'make' under hybrid/test and hybrid/qemu_plugin"
    exit 77
  fi
done

# 128 MB matches the QEMU virt machine default; vsim must use the same size.
SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-rt-shared.XXXXXX)

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Tier-2 round-trip e2e: cross-simulator memory survival via shared mmap.
#
# QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2 all map the same backing file. Phase A writes a
# pattern in QEMU 1; Phase B verifies + mutates in vsim; Phase C re-reads
# in QEMU 2. Memory must survive both handoffs.
#
# Required environment:
#   VSIM_BIN     path to sim_<cpu> binary (host-runnable)
#   VSIM_QEMU    path to qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  path to libhybrid_handoff.so

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

FIXTURE=rt_shared_mem
EXPECT_RC=0

VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/${FIXTURE}.elf}"

for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built; run 'make' under hybrid/test and hybrid/qemu_plugin"
    exit 77
  fi
done

# 128 MB matches the QEMU virt machine default; vsim must use the same size.
SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-rt-shared.XXXXXX)
STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM" "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT

truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"

echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3] QEMU 1 (shared mmap): drain entry state"
"$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
  -machine virt,memory-backend=mem \
  -bios none -nographic \
  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_QEMU" >/dev/null 2>&1

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 2/3] vsim (shared mmap): resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2"
set +e
timeout 60 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --shared-mem-path "$SHARED_MEM" \
  --shared-mem-size "$SHARED_SIZE" \
  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" != "$EXPECT_RC" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: expected exit $EXPECT_RC, got $RC"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 3/3] PASS: round-trip exit code $RC"

cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.20)
project(hybrid_sim NONE)

# The hybrid_sim workspace owns the wire ABI (include/hybrid/), the QEMU TCG
# plugin (qemu_plugin/), the bare-metal RV64 fixtures (fixtures/), and the
# end-to-end shell drivers (tests/). It pulls in hybrid_vsim as a CMake
# subproject and forwards HYBRID_DRIVER_DIR so vsim resolves the wire-ABI
# headers from this workspace's include/ instead of its own (now-removed)
# in-tree copy.
#
# QEMU is built outside CMake (autotools/meson) via build.sh's `qemu`
# subcommand. We do not subsume that build here.

include(GNUInstallDirs)
enable_testing()

# Single source of truth for hybrid co-sim wire artifacts. hybrid_vsim's
# CMakeLists.txt reads this cache var and uses ${HYBRID_DRIVER_DIR}/include
# as the include path for code that pulls in hybrid/state_abi.h etc.
set(HYBRID_DRIVER_DIR "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}" CACHE PATH
    "Path to the hybrid_sim workspace root (provides include/hybrid/, qemu_plugin/, fixtures/, tests/)")

# Forward the QEMU root and turn on hybrid features so vsim's CMake registers
# its hybrid unit tests against this workspace.
set(VSIM_QEMU_ROOT "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/hybrid_qemu" CACHE PATH "" FORCE)
set(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID ON CACHE BOOL "" FORCE)

add_subdirectory(hybrid_vsim)

include(cmake/HybridFixtures.cmake)
include(cmake/HybridPlugin.cmake)
include(cmake/HybridE2E.cmake)

# Bare-metal RV64 fixture ELFs used by the hybrid e2e tests.
# Wraps fixtures/Makefile (which already encodes the toolchain + linker
# scripts) so the top-level build can drive fixture builds without
# duplicating the recipe.

set(HYBRID_FIXTURES_DIR "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/fixtures")

set(HYBRID_FIXTURE_NAMES
  spin
  handoff
  handoff_exit
  handoff_roundtrip
  rt_all_gprs
  rt_abi_aliases
  rt_sign_bits
  rt_pc_jump
  rt_long_roi
  rt_mem_loadstore
  rt_unaligned_lh
  rt_shared_mem
  rt_csr_survival
)

set(_fixture_outputs)
foreach(name ${HYBRID_FIXTURE_NAMES})
  list(APPEND _fixture_outputs "${HYBRID_FIXTURES_DIR}/${name}.elf")
endforeach()

add_custom_command(
  OUTPUT ${_fixture_outputs}
  COMMAND $(MAKE) -C "${HYBRID_FIXTURES_DIR}" all
  WORKING_DIRECTORY "${HYBRID_FIXTURES_DIR}"
  COMMENT "Building hybrid RV64 fixture ELFs"
  VERBATIM
)

add_custom_target(hybrid_fixtures ALL DEPENDS ${_fixture_outputs})

input.content:
# End-to-end ctest registrations for the hybrid co-simulation harness.
# Each shell driver under tests/ skips with exit 77 when QEMU/plugin/ELFs
# are missing, so a fresh-clone ctest run does not FAIL even before
# `./build.sh` has produced artifacts.

set(HYBRID_TESTS_DIR "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/tests")

# Tier-1: each pair maps a fixture name (also the bash FIXTURE env var) to
# the expected vsim process exit code. The handoff_roundtrip fixture's
# original observable is "a2 == 3 -> exit 3"; round-trip rt_* fixtures
# encode pass/fail into a checksum that semihosts as 0 on success.
set(HYBRID_RT_FIXTURES
  "handoff_roundtrip:3"
  "rt_pc_jump:0"
  "rt_sign_bits:0"
  "rt_abi_aliases:0"
  "rt_long_roi:0"
  "rt_all_gprs:0"
  "rt_mem_loadstore:0"
  "rt_unaligned_lh:0"
  "rt_csr_survival:0"
)
foreach(entry ${HYBRID_RT_FIXTURES})
  string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
  list(GET parts 0 fname)
  list(GET parts 1 erc)
  add_test(
    NAME test_e2e_${fname}
    COMMAND bash -c
      "FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ${HYBRID_TESTS_DIR}/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  )
  set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
    SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
    LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
  )
endforeach()

# Tier-2: shared-mem driver allocates /dev/shm and uses --shared-mem-path,
# so it has its own driver instead of going through roundtrip_e2e.sh.
add_test(NAME test_e2e_rt_shared_mem
  COMMAND bash "${HYBRID_TESTS_DIR}/rt_shared_mem.sh")
set_tests_properties(test_e2e_rt_shared_mem PROPERTIES
  SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
  LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
)

# Negative / robustness drivers.
foreach(neg corrupt_state no_qemu_binary gdbstub_timeout)
  add_test(NAME test_e2e_rt_${neg}
    COMMAND bash "${HYBRID_TESTS_DIR}/rt_${neg}.sh")
  set_tests_properties(test_e2e_rt_${neg} PROPERTIES
    SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
    LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
  )
endforeach()

# End-to-end ctest registrations for the hybrid co-simulation harness.
# Each shell driver under tests/ skips with exit 77 when QEMU/plugin/ELFs
# are missing, so a fresh-clone ctest run does not FAIL even before
# `./build.sh` has produced artifacts.

set(HYBRID_TESTS_DIR "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/tests")

# Tier-1: each pair maps a fixture name (also the bash FIXTURE env var) to
# the expected vsim process exit code. The handoff_roundtrip fixture's
# original observable is "a2 == 3 -> exit 3"; round-trip rt_* fixtures
# encode pass/fail into a checksum that semihosts as 0 on success.
set(HYBRID_RT_FIXTURES
  "handoff_roundtrip:3"
  "rt_pc_jump:0"
  "rt_sign_bits:0"
  "rt_abi_aliases:0"
  "rt_long_roi:0"
  "rt_all_gprs:0"
  "rt_mem_loadstore:0"
  "rt_unaligned_lh:0"
  "rt_csr_survival:0"
)
foreach(entry ${HYBRID_RT_FIXTURES})
  string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
  list(GET parts 0 fname)
  list(GET parts 1 erc)
  add_test(
    NAME test_e2e_${fname}
    COMMAND bash -c
      "FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ${HYBRID_TESTS_DIR}/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  )
  set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
    SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
    LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
  )
endforeach()

# Tier-2: shared-mem driver allocates /dev/shm and uses --shared-mem-path,
# so it has its own driver instead of going through roundtrip_e2e.sh.
add_test(NAME test_e2e_rt_shared_mem
  COMMAND bash "${HYBRID_TESTS_DIR}/rt_shared_mem.sh")
set_tests_properties(test_e2e_rt_shared_mem PROPERTIES
  SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
  LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
)

# Negative / robustness drivers.
foreach(neg corrupt_state no_qemu_binary gdbstub_timeout)
  add_test(NAME test_e2e_rt_${neg}
    COMMAND bash "${HYBRID_TESTS_DIR}/rt_${neg}.sh")
  set_tests_properties(test_e2e_rt_${neg} PROPERTIES
    SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
    LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
  )
endforeach()

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Orchestrates the hybrid simulator build: QEMU 9.x + vsim + TCG plugin.
# Run subcommands individually, or run with no args to build everything.

set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
QEMU_DIR="$ROOT/hybrid_qemu"
VSIM_DIR="$ROOT/hybrid_vsim"
PLUGIN_DIR="$VSIM_DIR/hybrid/qemu_plugin"
QEMU_BUILD="$QEMU_DIR/build"
VSIM_BUILD="$VSIM_DIR/build"

JOBS="$(nproc)"

c_step()  { printf '\033[1;34m==>\033[0m \033[1m%s\033[0m\n' "$*"; }
c_ok()    { printf '\033[1;32m[ok]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
c_err()   { printf '\033[1;31m[err]\033[0m %s\n' "$*" >&2; }

require_dir() {
  if [[ ! -d "$1" ]]; then
    c_err "missing $1 - did you run \`git submodule update --init --recursive\`?"
    exit 1
  fi
}

build_qemu() {
  require_dir "$QEMU_DIR"
  c_step "QEMU: configure (if needed)"
  if [[ ! -f "$QEMU_BUILD/build.ninja" && ! -f "$QEMU_BUILD/Makefile" ]]; then
    mkdir -p "$QEMU_BUILD"
    (cd "$QEMU_BUILD" && ../configure \
      --target-list=riscv64-softmmu \
      --enable-plugins \
      --disable-werror)
  fi
  c_step "QEMU: build qemu-system-riscv64"
  if [[ -f "$QEMU_BUILD/build.ninja" ]]; then
    ninja -C "$QEMU_BUILD" qemu-system-riscv64
  else
    make -C "$QEMU_BUILD" -j"$JOBS" qemu-system-riscv64
  fi
  c_ok "QEMU built: $QEMU_BUILD/qemu-system-riscv64"
}

build_vsim() {
  require_dir "$VSIM_DIR"
  c_step "vsim: configure (if needed)"
  if [[ ! -f "$VSIM_BUILD/CMakeCache.txt" ]]; then
    cmake -S "$VSIM_DIR" -B "$VSIM_BUILD" -G Ninja \
      -DVSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON \
      -DVSIM_QEMU_ROOT="$QEMU_DIR"
  fi
  c_step "vsim: build"
  cmake --build "$VSIM_BUILD" -j"$JOBS"
  c_ok "vsim built: $VSIM_BUILD/vsim"
}

build_plugin() {
  require_dir "$PLUGIN_DIR"
  c_step "plugin: build libhybrid_handoff.so"
  make -C "$PLUGIN_DIR" QEMU_ROOT="$QEMU_DIR"
  c_ok "plugin built: $PLUGIN_DIR/libhybrid_handoff.so"
}

run_tests() {
  require_dir "$VSIM_BUILD"
  c_step "ctest: hybrid-e2e label"
  (cd "$VSIM_BUILD" && ctest -L hybrid-e2e --output-on-failure)
}

clean_all() {
  c_step "clean: remove build artifacts"
  rm -rf "$QEMU_BUILD" "$VSIM_BUILD"
  rm -f  "$PLUGIN_DIR/libhybrid_handoff.so"
  c_ok "cleaned"
}

usage() {
  cat <<EOF
usage: $(basename "$0") [qemu|vsim|plugin|test|clean|all]

  (no args)  same as 'all'
  qemu       configure + build hybrid_qemu/build/qemu-system-riscv64
  vsim       configure + build hybrid_vsim/build/vsim (VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON)
  plugin     build hybrid_vsim/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so
  test       ctest -L hybrid-e2e (in vsim build dir)
  clean      remove both build dirs and the plugin .so
  all        qemu, then vsim, then plugin (in this order)
EOF
}

cmd="${1:-all}"
case "$cmd" in
  qemu)   build_qemu ;;
  vsim)   build_vsim ;;
  plugin) build_plugin ;;
  test)   run_tests ;;
  clean)  clean_all ;;
  all)    build_qemu; build_vsim; build_plugin ;;
  -h|--help|help) usage ;;
  *)      c_err "unknown command: $cmd"; usage; exit 2 ;;
esac

input.content:
# hybrid_sim

Workspace for the Andes hybrid simulator: QEMU 9.x (functional, MHz) + vsim
(SystemC + Verilator, cycle-accurate) sharing memory and architectural state
across a magic-CSR boundary.

The driver itself - wire ABI, QEMU TCG plugin, RV64 fixtures, e2e harness -
lives at this top level. Two submodules provide the underlying simulators:

- `hybrid_qemu/` - QEMU fork (built outside CMake; autotools/meson)
- `hybrid_vsim/` - vsim sources (built via the top-level CMake project)

## Quickstart

```sh
git clone --recursive https://gitea.andestech.com/nick/hybrid_sim
cd hybrid_sim
./build.sh           # qemu, then top-level cmake --build (vsim + plugin + fixtures)
./build.sh test      # ctest -L hybrid-e2e (under build/)
```

Subcommands: `qemu`, `vsim`, `plugin`, `fixtures`, `test`, `clean`. Run
`./build.sh -h` for the full list.

A direct CMake invocation is equivalent for everything except QEMU:

```sh
./build.sh qemu                           # bootstraps QEMU once
cmake -S . -B build -GNinja
cmake --build build -j$(nproc)
ctest --test-dir build -L hybrid-e2e
```

## Host dependencies

- C/C++ toolchain (clang preferred), CMake >= 3.20, ninja
- Python 3
- glib-2.0 dev (for the QEMU plugin)
- pkg-config
- Verilator (auto-detected; set `VSIM_VERILATOR_EXECUTABLE` if needed)
- libraries QEMU itself needs (pixman, slirp, etc. - see `hybrid_qemu/README.rst`)

## Layout

| Path | Role |
|------|------|
| `include/hybrid/` | wire-ABI headers shared by QEMU plugin (C) and vsim (C++) |
| `qemu_plugin/` | `libhybrid_handoff.so` source + Makefile (links QEMU + glib) |
| `fixtures/` | bare-metal RV64 e2e fixtures (`.S`, `.ld`, Makefile) |
| `tests/` | end-to-end shell drivers (`roundtrip_e2e.sh`, `rt_*.sh`) |
| `cmake/` | top-level `Hybrid{Plugin,Fixtures,E2E}.cmake` |
| `hybrid_qemu/` | QEMU fork (submodule) |
| `hybrid_vsim/` | vsim simulator (submodule); `src/hybrid/` is its hybrid integration adapter |

## Outputs

After `./build.sh`:

- `hybrid_qemu/build/qemu-system-riscv64`
- `build/hybrid_vsim/sim_<cpu>` (e.g. `sim_ax45mpv_premium`)
- `qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so`
- `fixtures/*.elf`

## Quickstart

```sh
git clone --recursive https://gitea.andestech.com/nick/hybrid_sim
cd hybrid_sim
./build.sh           # qemu, vsim, plugin (in that order)
```

content.content:
1	# CLAUDE.md
2	
3	This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
4	
5	## Repository purpose
6	
7	Workspace for the **Andes hybrid simulator**: QEMU 9.x (functional, MHz) + vsim (SystemC + Verilator, cycle-accurate) sharing memory and architectural state across a magic-CSR boundary. The intent is to run boot/libc/warm-up in QEMU at MHz, then hand off to vsim for the cycle-accurate region of interest (ROI), with optional hand-back to QEMU.
8	
9	This top-level repo is a **thin orchestrator** over two submodules pinned to the `hybrid` branch:
10	
11	- `hybrid_qemu/` - QEMU 9.x fork; the TCG plugin builds against its headers.
12	- `hybrid_vsim/` - vsim source tree. All hybrid logic, fixtures, and tests live under `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/` and `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/`. The detailed design doc is `hybrid_vsim/hybrid_plan.md` (read this for any non-trivial change to the handoff path).
13	
14	## Common commands
15	
16	`build.sh` is the single entry point. It hardcodes the inter-component layout (QEMU root, vsim CMake flags, plugin Makefile) so do not call sub-builds directly unless iterating.
17	
18	```sh
19	./build.sh           # qemu, vsim, plugin (in this order; required ordering)
20	./build.sh qemu      # just QEMU (configure if needed, then ninja qemu-system-riscv64)
21	./build.sh vsim      # just vsim (CMake -DVSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON -DVSIM_QEMU_ROOT=$ROOT/hybrid_qemu)
22	./build.sh plugin    # build hybrid_vsim/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so out-of-tree
23	./build.sh test      # cd hybrid_vsim/build && ctest -L hybrid-e2e --output-on-failure
24	./build.sh clean     # rm -rf both build/ dirs and the plugin .so
25	```
26	
27	Run a single e2e fixture (after a full build):
28	
29	```sh
30	cd hybrid_vsim/build && ctest -R test_e2e_rt_all_gprs --output-on-failure
31	```
32	
33	Build the test ELFs and the plugin manually when iterating on fixtures:
34	
35	```sh
36	make -C hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test                                    # rt_*.elf, handoff*.elf
37	make -C hybrid_vsim/hybrid/qemu_plugin QEMU_ROOT=$PWD/hybrid_qemu  # libhybrid_handoff.so
38	```
39	
40	## Architecture: how a hybrid run flows
41	
42	A round-trip exercises three processes sharing a single packed `struct hybrid_state_v1` (defined in `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/include/state_abi.h`, with `static_assert` offsets in `state_abi_check.h`):
43	
44	1. **QEMU 1** runs with `libhybrid_handoff.so` loaded via `-plugin`. The plugin matches `csrrw rd,0x7C0,rs1` (mask `0xfff03073`); on `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` it drains GPR/FP/CSR via `qemu_plugin_read_register()`, writes the ABI struct to `outfile=`, and calls `qemu_plugin_request_exit()`.
45	2. **vsim** starts with `--resume-from-qemu <state.bin>`. Resume is driven **in-process** through the existing JTAG/DTM (`hybrid_vsim/src/platform/jtag_dtm.hpp`): halt -> Abstract Commands write x1..x31 + DPC -> `dret`. No DPI shim, no OpenOCD socket. vsim then runs cycle-accurately until a retire of a pre-scanned `csrw 0x7C0, 1` PC fires `CsrTrigger`, drains state again via DM, writes `--handoff-out`, and exits with `EXIT_HANDOFF_TO_QEMU = 200`.
46	3. **vsim's main** (on exit-200, when `--qemu-binary` is set) spawns **QEMU 2** with `-gdb -S -semihosting`. `qemu_handback.hpp` connects to the gdbstub, restores GPRs+PC, sends `c`, and the program completes via the RISC-V semihosting `ebreak`-triple.
47	
48	Memory is **shared, not copied**: when `--shared-mem-path` is given, vsim's `SimpleMemory` is backed by an mmap of `/dev/shm/...` and QEMU is launched with `-object memory-backend-file,share=on,mem-path=...`. To make the file offsets agree, vsim's RAM region is **narrowed** in hybrid mode to `[0x80000000, 0x80000000+N)` (matching QEMU virt's `VIRT_DRAM`) instead of the default `[0x0, 0x8FFFFFFF]`. See `hybrid_plan.md` Section 9 - touching `simple_memory.hpp` / `platform.hpp` / `mmap_backing.hpp` without re-reading that section will likely re-break the offset contract.
49	
50	Microarchitectural state (TLB, caches, BPU, store buffer) is **discarded** at handoff; the existing `--kanata-loop-skip N` mechanism warms up before recording.
51	
52	## Critical files (most changes touch these)
53	
54	`hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/`:
55	- `csr_trigger.hpp` - retire-PC watcher; pre-scans the ELF for `csrw 0x7C0, *` PCs.
56	- `handoff_controller.hpp` - decodes `csrrw` form, dispatches enter (rs1==0) vs runtime-exit (rs1==1).
57	- `state_drain.hpp` / `resume_driver.hpp` - DM Abstract-Command read/write of GPRs+DPC.
58	- `qemu_handback.hpp` - spawns QEMU 2; builds `-object memory-backend-file` argv when `shared_mem_path` is set.
59	- `gdbstub_client.hpp` / `gdbstub_session.hpp` - in-process gdbstub used to push state into QEMU 2.
60	- `resume_from_qemu.hpp`, `state_loader.hpp` - ABI-struct ingest on the vsim side.
61	- `exit_codes.hpp` - `EXIT_HANDOFF_TO_QEMU = 200` and friends.
62	
63	`hybrid_vsim/hybrid/`:
64	- `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c` + `Makefile` - the only thing that links against QEMU headers; built out-of-tree.
65	- `include/hybrid/{state_abi.h,state_abi_check.h,insn_match.h,reg_names.h}` - shared by C plugin and C++ vsim.
66	- `test/*.S` + `handoff*.ld` - bare-metal RV64 fixtures using the three-phase template (Phase A in QEMU 1 -> `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` -> Phase B in vsim -> `csrwi 0x7C0, 1` -> Phase C in QEMU 2 -> semihosting `SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED`). Pass/fail signal is the QEMU 2 exit code.
67	
68	`hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` registers all hybrid unit tests (always built) plus the `hybrid-e2e` ctest label (only when `VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON`). Each fixture is one CTest case; missing artifacts cause `exit 77` (skip), not failure.
69	
70	## Conventions specific to this repo
71	
72	- **Submodules pinned to `hybrid` branch**: never push submodule changes from this superproject without first pushing the corresponding branch in the submodule's own remote.
73	- **Three-phase fixture template**: every new `rt_*.S` fixture must encode pass/fail into a GPR before `csrwi 0x7C0, 1`, then verify in Phase C and ship the result through semihosting `SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED`. FP/CSR/V state cannot be used as test channels - they are not yet round-tripped (Phase 5 work).
74	- **Hybrid state ABI is versioned**: `state_abi.h` carries magic `'AHYB'` + version; offsets are `static_assert`-checked in `state_abi_check.h`. Any layout change requires bumping the version and updating both the C plugin and C++ loaders in the same commit.
75	- **What is intentionally out of scope inside a ROI**: MMIO, lr/sc reservations across handoff, self-modifying code, and `mcycle`/`minstret` continuity. Don't add fallback paths for these without a Phase-5 plan.
76

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Repository purpose

Workspace for the **Andes hybrid simulator**: QEMU 9.x (functional, MHz) + vsim (SystemC + Verilator, cycle-accurate) sharing memory and architectural state across a magic-CSR boundary. The intent is to run boot/libc/warm-up in QEMU at MHz, then hand off to vsim for the cycle-accurate region of interest (ROI), with optional hand-back to QEMU.

This top-level repo is a **thin orchestrator** over two submodules pinned to the `hybrid` branch:

- `hybrid_qemu/` - QEMU 9.x fork; the TCG plugin builds against its headers.
- `hybrid_vsim/` - vsim source tree. All hybrid logic, fixtures, and tests live under `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/` and `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/`. The detailed design doc is `hybrid_vsim/hybrid_plan.md` (read this for any non-trivial change to the handoff path).

## Common commands

`build.sh` is the single entry point. It hardcodes the inter-component layout (QEMU root, vsim CMake flags, plugin Makefile) so do not call sub-builds directly unless iterating.

```sh
./build.sh           # qemu, vsim, plugin (in this order; required ordering)
./build.sh qemu      # just QEMU (configure if needed, then ninja qemu-system-riscv64)
./build.sh vsim      # just vsim (CMake -DVSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON -DVSIM_QEMU_ROOT=$ROOT/hybrid_qemu)
./build.sh plugin    # build hybrid_vsim/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so out-of-tree
./build.sh test      # cd hybrid_vsim/build && ctest -L hybrid-e2e --output-on-failure
./build.sh clean     # rm -rf both build/ dirs and the plugin .so
```

Run a single e2e fixture (after a full build):

```sh
cd hybrid_vsim/build && ctest -R test_e2e_rt_all_gprs --output-on-failure
```

Build the test ELFs and the plugin manually when iterating on fixtures:

```sh
make -C hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test                                    # rt_*.elf, handoff*.elf
make -C hybrid_vsim/hybrid/qemu_plugin QEMU_ROOT=$PWD/hybrid_qemu  # libhybrid_handoff.so
```

## Architecture: how a hybrid run flows

A round-trip exercises three processes sharing a single packed `struct hybrid_state_v1` (defined in `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/include/state_abi.h`, with `static_assert` offsets in `state_abi_check.h`):

1. **QEMU 1** runs with `libhybrid_handoff.so` loaded via `-plugin`. The plugin matches `csrrw rd,0x7C0,rs1` (mask `0xfff03073`); on `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` it drains GPR/FP/CSR via `qemu_plugin_read_register()`, writes the ABI struct to `outfile=`, and calls `qemu_plugin_request_exit()`.
2. **vsim** starts with `--resume-from-qemu <state.bin>`. Resume is driven **in-process** through the existing JTAG/DTM (`hybrid_vsim/src/platform/jtag_dtm.hpp`): halt -> Abstract Commands write x1..x31 + DPC -> `dret`. No DPI shim, no OpenOCD socket. vsim then runs cycle-accurately until a retire of a pre-scanned `csrw 0x7C0, 1` PC fires `CsrTrigger`, drains state again via DM, writes `--handoff-out`, and exits with `EXIT_HANDOFF_TO_QEMU = 200`.
3. **vsim's main** (on exit-200, when `--qemu-binary` is set) spawns **QEMU 2** with `-gdb -S -semihosting`. `qemu_handback.hpp` connects to the gdbstub, restores GPRs+PC, sends `c`, and the program completes via the RISC-V semihosting `ebreak`-triple.

Memory is **shared, not copied**: when `--shared-mem-path` is given, vsim's `SimpleMemory` is backed by an mmap of `/dev/shm/...` and QEMU is launched with `-object memory-backend-file,share=on,mem-path=...`. To make the file offsets agree, vsim's RAM region is **narrowed** in hybrid mode to `[0x80000000, 0x80000000+N)` (matching QEMU virt's `VIRT_DRAM`) instead of the default `[0x0, 0x8FFFFFFF]`. See `hybrid_plan.md` Section 9 - touching `simple_memory.hpp` / `platform.hpp` / `mmap_backing.hpp` without re-reading that section will likely re-break the offset contract.

Microarchitectural state (TLB, caches, BPU, store buffer) is **discarded** at handoff; the existing `--kanata-loop-skip N` mechanism warms up before recording.

## Critical files (most changes touch these)

`hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/`:
- `csr_trigger.hpp` - retire-PC watcher; pre-scans the ELF for `csrw 0x7C0, *` PCs.
- `handoff_controller.hpp` - decodes `csrrw` form, dispatches enter (rs1==0) vs runtime-exit (rs1==1).
- `state_drain.hpp` / `resume_driver.hpp` - DM Abstract-Command read/write of GPRs+DPC.
- `qemu_handback.hpp` - spawns QEMU 2; builds `-object memory-backend-file` argv when `shared_mem_path` is set.
- `gdbstub_client.hpp` / `gdbstub_session.hpp` - in-process gdbstub used to push state into QEMU 2.
- `resume_from_qemu.hpp`, `state_loader.hpp` - ABI-struct ingest on the vsim side.
- `exit_codes.hpp` - `EXIT_HANDOFF_TO_QEMU = 200` and friends.

`hybrid_vsim/hybrid/`:
- `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c` + `Makefile` - the only thing that links against QEMU headers; built out-of-tree.
- `include/hybrid/{state_abi.h,state_abi_check.h,insn_match.h,reg_names.h}` - shared by C plugin and C++ vsim.
- `test/*.S` + `handoff*.ld` - bare-metal RV64 fixtures using the three-phase template (Phase A in QEMU 1 -> `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` -> Phase B in vsim -> `csrwi 0x7C0, 1` -> Phase C in QEMU 2 -> semihosting `SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED`). Pass/fail signal is the QEMU 2 exit code.

`hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` registers all hybrid unit tests (always built) plus the `hybrid-e2e` ctest label (only when `VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON`). Each fixture is one CTest case; missing artifacts cause `exit 77` (skip), not failure.

## Conventions specific to this repo

- **Submodules pinned to `hybrid` branch**: never push submodule changes from this superproject without first pushing the corresponding branch in the submodule's own remote.
- **Three-phase fixture template**: every new `rt_*.S` fixture must encode pass/fail into a GPR before `csrwi 0x7C0, 1`, then verify in Phase C and ship the result through semihosting `SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED`. FP/CSR/V state cannot be used as test channels - they are not yet round-tripped (Phase 5 work).
- **Hybrid state ABI is versioned**: `state_abi.h` carries magic `'AHYB'` + version; offsets are `static_assert`-checked in `state_abi_check.h`. Any layout change requires bumping the version and updating both the C plugin and C++ loaders in the same commit.
- **What is intentionally out of scope inside a ROI**: MMIO, lr/sc reservations across handoff, self-modifying code, and `mcycle`/`minstret` continuity. Don't add fallback paths for these without a Phase-5 plan.

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Repository purpose

Workspace for the **Andes hybrid simulator**: QEMU 9.x (functional, MHz) + vsim (SystemC + Verilator, cycle-accurate) sharing memory and architectural state across a magic-CSR boundary. The intent is to run boot/libc/warm-up in QEMU at MHz, then hand off to vsim for the cycle-accurate region of interest (ROI), with optional hand-back to QEMU.

This top-level repo **owns the hybrid co-sim driver itself** (the wire ABI, the QEMU plugin, the bare-metal fixtures, and the e2e harness). Two submodules pinned to branch `hybrid` provide the underlying simulators:

- `hybrid_qemu/` - QEMU 9.x fork; built outside CMake (autotools/meson). The TCG plugin in `qemu_plugin/` links against its headers.
- `hybrid_vsim/` - vsim source tree, built via the top-level CMake project (`add_subdirectory(hybrid_vsim)` from `CMakeLists.txt`). The vsim-internal hybrid integration adapter lives at `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/` and includes the wire-ABI headers from this workspace's `include/hybrid/` via the `HYBRID_DRIVER_DIR` cache variable. The detailed design doc is `hybrid_vsim/hybrid_plan.md` (read this for any non-trivial change to the handoff path).

## Common commands

`build.sh` is the convenience entry point. Internally everything except QEMU goes through the top-level CMake project:

```sh
./build.sh            # qemu, then full top-level cmake --build (vsim + plugin + fixtures)
./build.sh qemu       # just QEMU (configure if needed, then ninja qemu-system-riscv64)
./build.sh vsim       # cmake --build (vsim binaries land under build/hybrid_vsim/)
./build.sh plugin     # cmake --build hybrid_handoff_plugin
./build.sh fixtures   # cmake --build hybrid_fixtures
./build.sh test       # ctest --test-dir build -L hybrid-e2e
./build.sh clean      # rm -rf hybrid_qemu/build, build/, plugin .so, fixture artifacts
```

Equivalent direct CMake invocation (after one-time `./build.sh qemu`):

```sh
cmake -S . -B build -GNinja
cmake --build build -j$(nproc)
ctest --test-dir build -L hybrid-e2e --output-on-failure
```

Run a single e2e fixture: `ctest --test-dir build -R test_e2e_rt_all_gprs --output-on-failure`.

Iterate on fixtures or plugin without re-running cmake: `make -C fixtures` and `make -C qemu_plugin QEMU_ROOT=$PWD/hybrid_qemu`.

## Architecture: how a hybrid run flows

A round-trip exercises three processes sharing a single packed `struct hybrid_state_v1` (defined in `include/hybrid/state_abi.h`, with `static_assert` offsets in `state_abi_check.h`):

1. **QEMU 1** runs with `qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so` loaded via `-plugin`. The plugin matches `csrrw rd,0x7C0,rs1` (mask `0xfff03073`); on `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` it drains GPR/FP/CSR via `qemu_plugin_read_register()`, writes the ABI struct to `outfile=`, and calls `qemu_plugin_request_exit()`.
2. **vsim** starts with `--resume-from-qemu <state.bin>`. Resume is driven **in-process** through the existing JTAG/DTM (`hybrid_vsim/src/platform/jtag_dtm.hpp`): halt -> Abstract Commands write x1..x31 + DPC -> `dret`. No DPI shim, no OpenOCD socket. vsim then runs cycle-accurately until a retire of a pre-scanned `csrw 0x7C0, 1` PC fires `CsrTrigger`, drains state again via DM, writes `--handoff-out`, and exits with `EXIT_HANDOFF_TO_QEMU = 200`.
3. **vsim's main** (on exit-200, when `--qemu-binary` is set) spawns **QEMU 2** with `-gdb -S -semihosting`. `qemu_handback.hpp` connects to the gdbstub, restores GPRs+PC, sends `c`, and the program completes via the RISC-V semihosting `ebreak`-triple.

Memory is **shared, not copied**: when `--shared-mem-path` is given, vsim's `SimpleMemory` is backed by an mmap of `/dev/shm/...` and QEMU is launched with `-object memory-backend-file,share=on,mem-path=...`. To make the file offsets agree, vsim's RAM region is **narrowed** in hybrid mode to `[0x80000000, 0x80000000+N)` (matching QEMU virt's `VIRT_DRAM`) instead of the default `[0x0, 0x8FFFFFFF]`. See `hybrid_plan.md` Section 9 - touching `simple_memory.hpp` / `platform.hpp` / `mmap_backing.hpp` without re-reading that section will likely re-break the offset contract.

Microarchitectural state (TLB, caches, BPU, store buffer) is **discarded** at handoff; the existing `--kanata-loop-skip N` mechanism warms up before recording.

## Critical files (most changes touch these)

Top-level driver assets:

- `include/hybrid/{state_abi.h,state_abi_check.h,insn_match.h,reg_names.h}` - shared wire-ABI; one source of truth for the QEMU plugin and vsim.
- `qemu_plugin/{hybrid_handoff.c,Makefile}` - QEMU TCG plugin. Standalone Make build; `-I../include` resolves to `include/hybrid/`.
- `fixtures/*.S` + `handoff*.ld` + `Makefile` - bare-metal RV64 fixtures using the three-phase template (Phase A in QEMU 1 -> `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` -> Phase B in vsim -> `csrwi 0x7C0, 1` -> Phase C in QEMU 2 -> semihosting `SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED`). Pass/fail signal is the QEMU 2 exit code.
- `tests/{roundtrip_e2e,rt_*}.sh` + `qemu_silent_stub.py` - end-to-end shell drivers that orchestrate QEMU+vsim+QEMU. `REPO_ROOT=$SCRIPT_DIR/..` resolves to this workspace root; defaults look under `build/hybrid_vsim/sim_<cpu>`, `qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so`, `fixtures/<name>.elf`.
- `cmake/{HybridPlugin,HybridFixtures,HybridE2E}.cmake` - top-level CTest registrations and `add_custom_target` wrappers around the standalone Makefiles.

Vsim-internal integration (stays in the submodule):

- `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/`:
  - `csr_trigger.hpp` - retire-PC watcher; pre-scans the ELF for `csrw 0x7C0, *` PCs.
  - `handoff_controller.hpp` - decodes `csrrw` form, dispatches enter (rs1==0) vs runtime-exit (rs1==1).
  - `state_drain.hpp` / `resume_driver.hpp` - DM Abstract-Command read/write of GPRs+DPC.
  - `qemu_handback.hpp` - spawns QEMU 2; builds `-object memory-backend-file` argv when `shared_mem_path` is set.
  - `gdbstub_client.hpp` / `gdbstub_session.hpp` - in-process gdbstub used to push state into QEMU 2.
  - `resume_from_qemu.hpp`, `state_loader.hpp` - ABI-struct ingest on the vsim side.

- `hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` - registers vsim-linked hybrid unit tests; reads `${HYBRID_DRIVER_DIR}/include` for the wire-ABI include path.

## Conventions specific to this repo

- **Hybrid is baked into vsim's main.cpp** (not gated by `VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID`). vsim therefore requires `HYBRID_DRIVER_DIR` to be set to a directory containing `include/hybrid/state_abi.h`. The top-level CMake project does this automatically (`add_subdirectory(hybrid_vsim)` forwards `HYBRID_DRIVER_DIR=${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}`). Standalone `cmake -S hybrid_vsim ...` invocations need `-DHYBRID_DRIVER_DIR=/path/to/hybrid_sim` explicitly.
- **Submodules pinned to `hybrid` branch**: never push submodule changes from this superproject without first pushing the corresponding branch in the submodule's own remote.
- **Three-phase fixture template**: every new `rt_*.S` fixture must encode pass/fail into a GPR before `csrwi 0x7C0, 1`, then verify in Phase C and ship the result through semihosting `SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED`. FP/CSR/V state cannot be used as test channels - they are not yet round-tripped (Phase 5 work).
- **Hybrid state ABI is versioned**: `state_abi.h` carries magic `'AHYB'` + version; offsets are `static_assert`-checked in `state_abi_check.h`. Any layout change requires bumping the version and updating both the C plugin and C++ loaders in the same commit.
- **What is intentionally out of scope inside a ROI**: MMIO, lr/sc reservations across handoff, self-modifying code, and `mcycle`/`minstret` continuity. Don't add fallback paths for these without a Phase-5 plan.

-A round-trip exercises three processes sharing a single packed `struct hybrid_state_v1` (defined in `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/include/state_abi.h`, with `static_assert` offsets in `state_abi_check.h`):
+A round-trip exercises three processes sharing a single packed `struct hybrid_state_v1` (defined in `include/hybrid/state_abi.h`, with `static_assert` offsets in `state_abi_check.h`):

-1. **QEMU 1** runs with `libhybrid_handoff.so` loaded via `-plugin`. The plugin matches `csrrw rd,0x7C0,rs1` (mask `0xfff03073`); on `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` it drains GPR/FP/CSR via `qemu_plugin_read_register()`, writes the ABI struct to `outfile=`, and calls `qemu_plugin_request_exit()`.

+1. **QEMU 1** runs with `qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so` loaded via `-plugin`. The plugin matches `csrrw rd,0x7C0,rs1` (mask `0xfff03073`); on `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` it drains GPR/FP/CSR via `qemu_plugin_read_register()`, writes the ABI struct to `outfile=`, and calls `qemu_plugin_request_exit()`.

2. **vsim** starts with `--resume-from-qemu <state.bin>`. Resume is driven **in-process** through the existing JTAG/DTM (`hybrid_vsim/src/platform/jtag_dtm.hpp`): halt -> Abstract Commands write x1..x31 + DPC -> `dret`. No DPI shim, no OpenOCD socket. vsim then runs cycle-accurately until a retire of a pre-scanned `csrw 0x7C0, 1` PC fires `CsrTrigger`, drains state again via DM, writes `--handoff-out`, and exits with `EXIT_HANDOFF_TO_QEMU = 200`.

3. **vsim's main** (on exit-200, when `--qemu-binary` is set) spawns **QEMU 2** with `-gdb -S -semihosting`. `qemu_handback.hpp` connects to the gdbstub, restores GPRs+PC, sends `c`, and the program completes via the RISC-V semihosting `ebreak`-triple.
-- `csr_trigger.hpp` - retire-PC watcher; pre-scans the ELF for `csrw 0x7C0, *` PCs.
-- `handoff_controller.hpp` - decodes `csrrw` form, dispatches enter (rs1==0) vs runtime-exit (rs1==1).
-- `qemu_handback.hpp` - spawns QEMU 2; builds `-object memory-backend-file` argv when `shared_mem_path` is set.
-- `gdbstub_client.hpp` / `gdbstub_session.hpp` - in-process gdbstub used to push state into QEMU 2.
-- `resume_from_qemu.hpp`, `state_loader.hpp` - ABI-struct ingest on the vsim side.
-- `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c` + `Makefile` - the only thing that links against QEMU headers; built out-of-tree.
-- `include/hybrid/{state_abi.h,state_abi_check.h,insn_match.h,reg_names.h}` - shared by C plugin and C++ vsim.

-- `test/*.S` + `handoff*.ld` - bare-metal RV64 fixtures using the three-phase template (Phase A in QEMU 1 -> `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` -> Phase B in vsim -> `csrwi 0x7C0, 1` -> Phase C in QEMU 2 -> semihosting `SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED`). Pass/fail signal is the QEMU 2 exit code.
+- `include/hybrid/{state_abi.h,state_abi_check.h,insn_match.h,reg_names.h}` - shared wire-ABI; one source of truth for the QEMU plugin and vsim.
+- `qemu_plugin/{hybrid_handoff.c,Makefile}` - QEMU TCG plugin. Standalone Make build; `-I../include` resolves to `include/hybrid/`.

+- `fixtures/*.S` + `handoff*.ld` + `Makefile` - bare-metal RV64 fixtures using the three-phase template (Phase A in QEMU 1 -> `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` -> Phase B in vsim -> `csrwi 0x7C0, 1` -> Phase C in QEMU 2 -> semihosting `SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED`). Pass/fail signal is the QEMU 2 exit code.

+- `tests/{roundtrip_e2e,rt_*}.sh` + `qemu_silent_stub.py` - end-to-end shell drivers that orchestrate QEMU+vsim+QEMU. `REPO_ROOT=$SCRIPT_DIR/..` resolves to this workspace root; defaults look under `build/hybrid_vsim/sim_<cpu>`, `qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so`, `fixtures/<name>.elf`.
+- `cmake/{HybridPlugin,HybridFixtures,HybridE2E}.cmake` - top-level CTest registrations and `add_custom_target` wrappers around the standalone Makefiles.

-`hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` registers all hybrid unit tests (always built) plus the `hybrid-e2e` ctest label (only when `VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON`). Each fixture is one CTest case; missing artifacts cause `exit 77` (skip), not failure.
+  - `csr_trigger.hpp` - retire-PC watcher; pre-scans the ELF for `csrw 0x7C0, *` PCs.
+  - `handoff_controller.hpp` - decodes `csrrw` form, dispatches enter (rs1==0) vs runtime-exit (rs1==1).
+  - `qemu_handback.hpp` - spawns QEMU 2; builds `-object memory-backend-file` argv when `shared_mem_path` is set.
+  - `gdbstub_client.hpp` / `gdbstub_session.hpp` - in-process gdbstub used to push state into QEMU 2.
+  - `resume_from_qemu.hpp`, `state_loader.hpp` - ABI-struct ingest on the vsim side.
+- `hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` - registers vsim-linked hybrid unit tests; reads `${HYBRID_DRIVER_DIR}/include` for the wire-ABI include path.

+- **Hybrid is baked into vsim's main.cpp** (not gated by `VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID`). vsim therefore requires `HYBRID_DRIVER_DIR` to be set to a directory containing `include/hybrid/state_abi.h`. The top-level CMake project does this automatically (`add_subdirectory(hybrid_vsim)` forwards `HYBRID_DRIVER_DIR=${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}`). Standalone `cmake -S hybrid_vsim ...` invocations need `-DHYBRID_DRIVER_DIR=/path/to/hybrid_sim` explicitly.
- **Submodules pinned to `hybrid` branch**: never push submodule changes from this superproject without first pushing the corresponding branch in the submodule's own remote.

- **Three-phase fixture template**: every new `rt_*.S` fixture must encode pass/fail into a GPR before `csrwi 0x7C0, 1`, then verify in Phase C and ship the result through semihosting `SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED`. FP/CSR/V state cannot be used as test channels - they are not yet round-tripped (Phase 5 work).

- **Hybrid state ABI is versioned**: `state_abi.h` carries magic `'AHYB'` + version; offsets are `static_assert`-checked in `state_abi_check.h`. Any layout change requires bumping the version and updating both the C plugin and C++ loaders in the same commit.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Repository purpose

Workspace for the **Andes hybrid simulator**: QEMU 9.x (functional, MHz) + vsim (SystemC + Verilator, cycle-accurate) sharing memory and architectural state across a magic-CSR boundary. The intent is to run boot/libc/warm-up in QEMU at MHz, then hand off to vsim for the cycle-accurate region of interest (ROI), with optional hand-back to QEMU.

This top-level repo is a **thin orchestrator** over two submodules pinned to the `hybrid` branch:

- `hybrid_qemu/` - QEMU 9.x fork; the TCG plugin builds against its headers.
- `hybrid_vsim/` - vsim source tree. All hybrid logic, fixtures, and tests live under `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/` and `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/`. The detailed design doc is `hybrid_vsim/hybrid_plan.md` (read this for any non-trivial change to the handoff path).

## Common commands

`build.sh` is the single entry point. It hardcodes the inter-component layout (QEMU root, vsim CMake flags, plugin Makefile) so do not call sub-builds directly unless iterating.

```sh
./build.sh           # qemu, vsim, plugin (in this order; required ordering)
./build.sh qemu      # just QEMU (configure if needed, then ninja qemu-system-riscv64)
./build.sh vsim      # just vsim (CMake -DVSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON -DVSIM_QEMU_ROOT=$ROOT/hybrid_qemu)
./build.sh plugin    # build hybrid_vsim/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so out-of-tree
./build.sh test      # cd hybrid_vsim/build && ctest -L hybrid-e2e --output-on-failure
./build.sh clean     # rm -rf both build/ dirs and the plugin .so
```

Run a single e2e fixture (after a full build):

```sh
cd hybrid_vsim/build && ctest -R test_e2e_rt_all_gprs --output-on-failure
```

Build the test ELFs and the plugin manually when iterating on fixtures:

```sh
make -C hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test                                    # rt_*.elf, handoff*.elf
make -C hybrid_vsim/hybrid/qemu_plugin QEMU_ROOT=$PWD/hybrid_qemu  # libhybrid_handoff.so
```

## Architecture: how a hybrid run flows

A round-trip exercises three processes sharing a single packed `struct hybrid_state_v1` (defined in `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/include/state_abi.h`, with `static_assert` offsets in `state_abi_check.h`):

1. **QEMU 1** runs with `libhybrid_handoff.so` loaded via `-plugin`. The plugin matches `csrrw rd,0x7C0,rs1` (mask `0xfff03073`); on `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` it drains GPR/FP/CSR via `qemu_plugin_read_register()`, writes the ABI struct to `outfile=`, and calls `qemu_plugin_request_exit()`.
2. **vsim** starts with `--resume-from-qemu <state.bin>`. Resume is driven **in-process** through the existing JTAG/DTM (`hybrid_vsim/src/platform/jtag_dtm.hpp`): halt -> Abstract Commands write x1..x31 + DPC -> `dret`. No DPI shim, no OpenOCD socket. vsim then runs cycle-accurately until a retire of a pre-scanned `csrw 0x7C0, 1` PC fires `CsrTrigger`, drains state again via DM, writes `--handoff-out`, and exits with `EXIT_HANDOFF_TO_QEMU = 200`.
3. **vsim's main** (on exit-200, when `--qemu-binary` is set) spawns **QEMU 2** with `-gdb -S -semihosting`. `qemu_handback.hpp` connects to the gdbstub, restores GPRs+PC, sends `c`, and the program completes via the RISC-V semihosting `ebreak`-triple.

Memory is **shared, not copied**: when `--shared-mem-path` is given, vsim's `SimpleMemory` is backed by an mmap of `/dev/shm/...` and QEMU is launched with `-object memory-backend-file,share=on,mem-path=...`. To make the file offsets agree, vsim's RAM region is **narrowed** in hybrid mode to `[0x80000000, 0x80000000+N)` (matching QEMU virt's `VIRT_DRAM`) instead of the default `[0x0, 0x8FFFFFFF]`. See `hybrid_plan.md` Section 9 - touching `simple_memory.hpp` / `platform.hpp` / `mmap_backing.hpp` without re-reading that section will likely re-break the offset contract.

Microarchitectural state (TLB, caches, BPU, store buffer) is **discarded** at handoff; the existing `--kanata-loop-skip N` mechanism warms up before recording.

## Critical files (most changes touch these)

`hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/`:
- `csr_trigger.hpp` - retire-PC watcher; pre-scans the ELF for `csrw 0x7C0, *` PCs.
- `handoff_controller.hpp` - decodes `csrrw` form, dispatches enter (rs1==0) vs runtime-exit (rs1==1).
- `state_drain.hpp` / `resume_driver.hpp` - DM Abstract-Command read/write of GPRs+DPC.
- `qemu_handback.hpp` - spawns QEMU 2; builds `-object memory-backend-file` argv when `shared_mem_path` is set.
- `gdbstub_client.hpp` / `gdbstub_session.hpp` - in-process gdbstub used to push state into QEMU 2.
- `resume_from_qemu.hpp`, `state_loader.hpp` - ABI-struct ingest on the vsim side.
- `exit_codes.hpp` - `EXIT_HANDOFF_TO_QEMU = 200` and friends.

`hybrid_vsim/hybrid/`:
- `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c` + `Makefile` - the only thing that links against QEMU headers; built out-of-tree.
- `include/hybrid/{state_abi.h,state_abi_check.h,insn_match.h,reg_names.h}` - shared by C plugin and C++ vsim.
- `test/*.S` + `handoff*.ld` - bare-metal RV64 fixtures using the three-phase template (Phase A in QEMU 1 -> `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` -> Phase B in vsim -> `csrwi 0x7C0, 1` -> Phase C in QEMU 2 -> semihosting `SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED`). Pass/fail signal is the QEMU 2 exit code.

`hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` registers all hybrid unit tests (always built) plus the `hybrid-e2e` ctest label (only when `VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON`). Each fixture is one CTest case; missing artifacts cause `exit 77` (skip), not failure.

## Conventions specific to this repo

- **Submodules pinned to `hybrid` branch**: never push submodule changes from this superproject without first pushing the corresponding branch in the submodule's own remote.
- **Three-phase fixture template**: every new `rt_*.S` fixture must encode pass/fail into a GPR before `csrwi 0x7C0, 1`, then verify in Phase C and ship the result through semihosting `SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED`. FP/CSR/V state cannot be used as test channels - they are not yet round-tripped (Phase 5 work).
- **Hybrid state ABI is versioned**: `state_abi.h` carries magic `'AHYB'` + version; offsets are `static_assert`-checked in `state_abi_check.h`. Any layout change requires bumping the version and updating both the C plugin and C++ loaders in the same commit.
- **What is intentionally out of scope inside a ROI**: MMIO, lr/sc reservations across handoff, self-modifying code, and `mcycle`/`minstret` continuity. Don't add fallback paths for these without a Phase-5 plan.
