cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.20)

# default to clang, the compiled binary runs faster than gcc after experiment
# need to be placed before project(...), because project(...) will decide
# which compiler to use
set(CMAKE_C_COMPILER clang CACHE STRING "C compiler")
set(CMAKE_CXX_COMPILER clang++ CACHE STRING "C++ compiler")

project(vsim LANGUAGES C CXX)

# these settings should be placed after project(...) i.e. after
# compiler is chosen
set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 20)
set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD_REQUIRED ON)
set(CMAKE_POSITION_INDEPENDENT_CODE ON)
set(CMAKE_EXPORT_COMPILE_COMMANDS ON)

# ##############################################################################
# Options
# ##############################################################################
option(VSIM_DEBUG "Enable VCD waveform tracing" OFF)
option(VSIM_DEBUG_FST "Enable FST waveform tracing (for long recordings)" OFF)
option(VSIM_SAVABLE "Enable save/resume simulation (increases build and run time)" OFF)
option(VSIM_NO_VERILATOR "Assume verilated modules are already built" OFF)

set(VSIM_CONFIG_FILE "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/config.yaml"
  CACHE FILEPATH "CPU/platform configuration YAML file")
set(VSIM_CCS_OPT ""
  CACHE STRING "Extra optimization flags for cpu_cluster_subsystem compilation")
set(VSIM_VERILATOR_EXECUTABLE ""
  CACHE FILEPATH "Path to verilator executable")
set(VSIM_VERILATOR_ROOT ""
  CACHE PATH "Verilator source root (auto-detected if empty)")
set(VSIM_CPUS ""
  CACHE STRING "Semicolon-separated list of CPUs to build (empty = all from config.yaml)")

if(VSIM_DEBUG AND VSIM_DEBUG_FST)
  message(FATAL_ERROR "VSIM_DEBUG and VSIM_DEBUG_FST are mutually exclusive")
endif()

# ##############################################################################
# Find Python (needed for config parsing)
# ##############################################################################
find_package(Python3 REQUIRED COMPONENTS Interpreter)

# ##############################################################################
# Find Verilator
# ##############################################################################
include(${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/cmake/FindVerilator.cmake)
include(${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/cmake/VerilatorHelpers.cmake)

    "top-level hybrid_sim CMake project.")
endif()

if(VSIM_DEBUG AND VSIM_DEBUG_FST)
  message(FATAL_ERROR "VSIM_DEBUG and VSIM_DEBUG_FST are mutually exclusive")
endif()

# Hybrid co-simulation: vsim-side unit-test registrations.
#
# The hybrid driver (wire ABI, QEMU TCG plugin, RV64 fixtures, e2e shell
# drivers) lives in the hybrid_sim parent workspace. This file only
# registers the C++ unit tests that link against vsim's libraries and need
# the wire-ABI headers via ${HYBRID_DRIVER_DIR}/include. The end-to-end
# CTest entries are owned by hybrid_sim/cmake/HybridE2E.cmake.

option(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID "Enable hybrid QEMU+Verilator co-simulation" OFF)

set(VSIM_QEMU_ROOT "/local/nick/qemu_v5"
  CACHE PATH "Path to QEMU source tree (used by the parent's HybridPlugin.cmake)")

# The ABI test is always built so struct layout drift is caught even in
# non-hybrid builds. It has no SystemC/Verilator dependency.
vsim_add_test(test_state_abi tests/hybrid/state_abi.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_abi PRIVATE
  "${HYBRID_DRIVER_DIR}/include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_abi)

# Shared-mem smoke: glues MmapBacking + SimpleMemory and verifies bytes are
# visible through an independent mmap of the same file.
vsim_add_test(test_shared_mem_smoke tests/hybrid/shared_mem_smoke.test.cpp)
target_link_libraries(test_shared_mem_smoke PRIVATE
  lib_fmt
  lib_spdlog
  lib_systemc
  ${VSIM_LINK_LIBS}
)
add_dependencies(test_shared_mem_smoke ext_fmt ext_spdlog ext_systemc)
add_dependencies(check test_shared_mem_smoke)

# Section 9: validates the narrowed QEMU-virt RAM region + mmap composition.
vsim_add_test(test_shared_mem_offset tests/hybrid/shared_mem_offset.test.cpp)
target_link_libraries(test_shared_mem_offset PRIVATE
  lib_fmt
  lib_spdlog
  lib_systemc
  ${VSIM_LINK_LIBS}
)
add_dependencies(test_shared_mem_offset ext_fmt ext_spdlog ext_systemc)
add_dependencies(check test_shared_mem_offset)

vsim_add_test(test_state_loader tests/hybrid/state_loader.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_loader PRIVATE
  "${HYBRID_DRIVER_DIR}/include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_loader)

vsim_add_test(test_abstract_command tests/hybrid/abstract_command.test.cpp)
add_dependencies(check test_abstract_command)

vsim_add_test(test_resume_driver tests/hybrid/resume_driver.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_resume_driver PRIVATE
  "${HYBRID_DRIVER_DIR}/include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_resume_driver)

vsim_add_test(test_insn_match tests/hybrid/insn_match.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_insn_match PRIVATE
  "${HYBRID_DRIVER_DIR}/include"
)

# Hybrid simulator (QEMU + Verilator) build integration.

option(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID "Enable hybrid QEMU+Verilator co-simulation" OFF)

set(VSIM_QEMU_ROOT "/local/nick/qemu_v5"
  CACHE PATH "Path to QEMU source tree used to build the hybrid TCG plugin")

# The ABI test is always built so struct layout drift is caught even in
# non-hybrid builds. It has no SystemC/Verilator dependency.
vsim_add_test(test_state_abi tests/hybrid/state_abi.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_abi PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/hybrid/include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_abi)

# Shared-mem smoke: glues MmapBacking + SimpleMemory and verifies bytes are
# visible through an independent mmap of the same file.
vsim_add_test(test_shared_mem_smoke tests/hybrid/shared_mem_smoke.test.cpp)
target_link_libraries(test_shared_mem_smoke PRIVATE
  lib_fmt
  lib_spdlog

  ${VSIM_LINK_LIBS}
)

# Section 9: validates the narrowed QEMU-virt RAM region + mmap composition.
vsim_add_test(test_shared_mem_offset tests/hybrid/shared_mem_offset.test.cpp)

  lib_fmt
  lib_spdlog
  lib_systemc
  ${VSIM_LINK_LIBS}
)
add_dependencies(test_shared_mem_offset ext_fmt ext_spdlog ext_systemc)
add_dependencies(check test_shared_mem_offset)

vsim_add_test(test_state_loader tests/hybrid/state_loader.test.cpp)

  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/hybrid/include"
)

vsim_add_test(test_abstract_command tests/hybrid/abstract_command.test.cpp)
add_dependencies(check test_abstract_command)

vsim_add_test(test_resume_driver tests/hybrid/resume_driver.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_resume_driver PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/hybrid/include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_resume_driver)

vsim_add_test(test_insn_match tests/hybrid/insn_match.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_insn_match PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/hybrid/include"

vsim_add_test(test_resume_from_qemu tests/hybrid/resume_from_qemu.test.cpp)

vsim_add_test(test_gdbstub_client tests/hybrid/gdbstub_client.test.cpp)
add_dependencies(check test_gdbstub_client)

vsim_add_test(test_handoff_controller tests/hybrid/handoff_controller.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_handoff_controller PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/hybrid/include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_handoff_controller)

if(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID)
  if(NOT EXISTS "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/qemu-plugin.h")
    message(WARNING
      "VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON but qemu-plugin.h not found under "
      "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/. Build the TCG plugin out-of-tree via "
      "hybrid/qemu_plugin/Makefile on a host that has QEMU installed.")
  endif()
  message(STATUS "Hybrid mode: enabled (QEMU root: ${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT})")

  # Round-trip e2e tests. Each entry registers one CTest case using the
  # parameterized roundtrip_e2e.sh harness. The harness skips (return 77)
  # when QEMU/plugin/ELF artifacts are missing, so the same CTestTestfile
  # works whether ctest is invoked from the container's /work/build or
  # from the host's build/.
  set(VSIM_RT_FIXTURES
    "handoff_roundtrip:3"
    "rt_pc_jump:0"
    "rt_sign_bits:0"
    "rt_abi_aliases:0"
    "rt_long_roi:0"
    "rt_all_gprs:0"
    "rt_mem_loadstore:0"
    "rt_unaligned_lh:0"
    "rt_csr_survival:0"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_FIXTURES})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 fname)
    list(GET parts 1 erc)
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
      COMMAND bash -c
        "FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
    )
  endforeach()

  # Tier-2: separate driver because it allocates /dev/shm and uses
  # --shared-mem-path / -object memory-backend-file to share RAM across
  # QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2.
  add_test(NAME test_e2e_rt_shared_mem
    COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh)
  set_tests_properties(test_e2e_rt_shared_mem PROPERTIES
    SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
    LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
  )

  # Negative / robustness drivers: each guards a quietly-degrading failure
  # path the happy-path round-trips can't see (corrupted state file,
  # missing --qemu-binary, silent gdbstub peer).
  foreach(neg corrupt_state no_qemu_binary gdbstub_timeout)
    add_test(NAME test_e2e_rt_${neg}
      COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_${neg}.sh)
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_rt_${neg} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
    )
  endforeach()
endif()

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# DM Abstract-Command coverage (vsim)

The hybrid handoff drives architectural state into the running RTL via the
in-process JTAG/DTM and the RISC-V Debug Module Abstract Commands. This page
records which `regno` ranges actually round-trip on each CPU variant.

A `MISS` row blocks the handoff for that field: either add a Program Buffer
or DPI fallback, or drop the field from the v1 ABI.

## How to populate this table

```bash
# in one terminal
./build/sim_ax45mpv_premium --jtag-port 9104 hello.elf

# in another terminal
openocd -f hybrid/scripts/test_dm_coverage.tcl 2>&1 \
  | tee /tmp/dm_coverage.log
```

Then translate the log into the table below.

## Coverage matrix

| Group              | Regnos              | Blocking? | ax45mpv_premium | ax66 | d45 |
| ------------------ | ------------------- | --------- | --------------- | ---- | --- |
| GPR x0..x31        | 0x1000-0x101F       | yes       | TBD             | TBD  | TBD |
| FPR f0..f31        | 0x1020-0x103F       | yes (FP)  | TBD             | TBD  | TBD |
| mstatus            | 0x300               | yes       | TBD             | TBD  | TBD |
| mepc/mtvec/mcause  | 0x341/0x305/0x342   | yes       | TBD             | TBD  | TBD |
| mtval/mscratch     | 0x343/0x340         | yes       | TBD             | TBD  | TBD |
| medeleg/mideleg    | 0x302/0x303         | yes       | TBD             | TBD  | TBD |
| mip/mie            | 0x344/0x304         | yes       | TBD             | TBD  | TBD |
| mcycle/minstret    | 0xB00/0xB02         | no        | TBD             | TBD  | TBD |
| satp               | 0x180               | yes       | TBD             | TBD  | TBD |
| pmpcfg0..15        | 0x3A0-0x3AF         | no        | TBD             | TBD  | TBD |
| pmpaddr0..63       | 0x3B0-0x3EF         | no        | TBD             | TBD  | TBD |
| V scalar CSRs      | 0x008,0xC20-0xC22   | no (V)    | TBD             | TBD  | TBD |
| V regfile v0..v31  | (no abstract regno) | no (V)    | n/a (PB)        | -    | -   |
| Andes mmsc_cfg     | 0xFC2               | no        | TBD             | TBD  | TBD |
| Andes mmisc_ctl    | 0x7D0               | no        | TBD             | TBD  | TBD |
| Andes mcache_ctl   | 0x7CA               | no        | TBD             | TBD  | TBD |

`PB` = Program Buffer fallback required (no abstract-regno path).
`Blocking?` = MISS on this row prevents the handoff from being correct without
a fallback.

# Probes which RISC-V Debug Module Abstract-Command regnos are reachable on
# the running vsim DM. Unreachable regnos block the hybrid handoff for that
# field unless we add a Program Buffer or DPI fallback.
#
# Usage:
#   1. Start vsim with --jtag-port 9104 and a small ELF.
#   2. openocd -f hybrid/scripts/test_dm_coverage.tcl 2>&1 | tee /tmp/dm_coverage.log
#   3. Translate the log into docs/HYBRID_DM_COVERAGE.md.
#
# Spec ref: RISC-V External Debug Support 1.0, table "Abstract Register Numbers".

adapter driver remote_bitbang
remote_bitbang_host localhost
remote_bitbang_port 9104

content.content: The file /home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/tools/dm_coverage.tcl has been updated successfully.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Probes which RISC-V Debug Module Abstract-Command regnos are reachable on
# the running vsim DM. Unreachable regnos block the hybrid handoff for that
# field unless we add a Program Buffer or DPI fallback.
#
# Usage:
#   1. Start vsim with --jtag-port 9104 and a small ELF.
#   2. openocd -f hybrid/scripts/test_dm_coverage.tcl 2>&1 | tee /tmp/dm_coverage.log
#   3. Translate the log into docs/HYBRID_DM_COVERAGE.md.
#
# Spec ref: RISC-V External Debug Support 1.0, table "Abstract Register Numbers".

adapter driver remote_bitbang
remote_bitbang_host localhost
remote_bitbang_port 9104

set _CHIPNAME riscv
jtag newtap $_CHIPNAME cpu -irlen 5 -expected-id 0x10e31913
set _TARGETNAME $_CHIPNAME.cpu
target create $_TARGETNAME riscv -chain-position $_TARGETNAME

riscv set_command_timeout_sec 5

if {[catch {init} err]} {
    puts "FATAL: DM init failed: $err"
    shutdown error
}
if {[catch {halt} err]} {
    puts "FATAL: hart halt failed: $err"
    shutdown error
}

# --- helpers -------------------------------------------------------------------
proc probe_reg {label regno} {
    # 'reg' addresses GPRs/CSRs by symbolic name on OpenOCD; for raw regno we
    # use the abstract command directly via 'riscv dmi_read/write' helpers.
    if {[catch {set v [riscv dm_read $regno]} err]} {
        puts "MISS  $label (regno=[format 0x%04x $regno]) : $err"
        return 0
    } else {
        puts "OK    $label (regno=[format 0x%04x $regno]) = $v"
        return 1
    }
}

# --- coverage matrix -----------------------------------------------------------
puts "=== GPR x0..x31 (regno 0x1000-0x101F) ==="
set gpr_ok 0
for {set i 0} {$i < 32} {incr i} {
    set gpr_ok [expr {$gpr_ok + [probe_reg "x$i" [expr {0x1000 + $i}]]}]
}
puts "GPR reachable: $gpr_ok / 32"

puts "\n=== FPR f0..f31 (regno 0x1020-0x103F) ==="
set fpr_ok 0
for {set i 0} {$i < 32} {incr i} {
    set fpr_ok [expr {$fpr_ok + [probe_reg "f$i" [expr {0x1020 + $i}]]}]
}
puts "FPR reachable: $fpr_ok / 32"

puts "\n=== Standard M-mode CSRs (regno = csr address) ==="
foreach {name addr} {
    mstatus  0x300
    misa     0x301
    medeleg  0x302
    mideleg  0x303
    mie      0x304
    mtvec    0x305
    mscratch 0x340
    mepc     0x341
    mcause   0x342
    mtval    0x343
    mip      0x344
    satp     0x180
    mcycle   0xb00
    minstret 0xb02
} {
    probe_reg $name $addr
}

puts "\n=== PMP (16 cfg + 64 addr CSRs) ==="
for {set i 0} {$i < 16} {incr i} {
    probe_reg "pmpcfg$i"  [expr {0x3a0 + $i}]
}
for {set i 0} {$i < 64} {incr i} {
    probe_reg "pmpaddr$i" [expr {0x3b0 + $i}]
}

puts "\n=== V-extension scalar CSRs ==="
foreach {name addr} {
    vstart 0x008
    vxsat  0x009
    vxrm   0x00a
    vcsr   0x00f
    vl     0xc20
    vtype  0xc21
    vlenb  0xc22
} {
    probe_reg $name $addr
}

puts "\n=== Andes-custom CSRs (sample; full list lives in andes-v5-priv-spec) ==="
foreach {name addr} {
    mmsc_cfg  0xfc2
    mmisc_ctl 0x7d0
    mcache_ctl 0x7ca
    mcctlbeginaddr 0x7cb
    mcctlcommand   0x7cc
    mcctldata      0x7cd
} {
    probe_reg $name $addr
}

puts "\n=== Vector regfile via Abstract Commands? (regno 0x1020 + offset is FPR; ==="
puts "===  V regs typically need Program Buffer load/store - testing scratch. ==="
# RISC-V Debug Spec does not assign abstract regnos to V regs; the path is
# Program Buffer. We only note the absence here; Phase 5 measures the cost.

shutdown

file.content:
# Out-of-tree build for the QEMU TCG plugin libhybrid_handoff.so. Standalone
# from the vsim CMake build because the plugin links against host QEMU's
# headers + glib, neither of which exist inside the vsim build container.
#
# Usage:
#   make QEMU_ROOT=/local/nick/qemu_v5
#
# Output: libhybrid_handoff.so in this directory.

QEMU_ROOT ?= /local/nick/qemu_v5
CC        ?= gcc

GLIB_CFLAGS := $(shell pkg-config --cflags glib-2.0)
GLIB_LIBS   := $(shell pkg-config --libs   glib-2.0)

CFLAGS  := -O2 -fPIC -Wall -Wextra -std=c11
INCLUDES := -I../include -I$(QEMU_ROOT)/include/qemu $(GLIB_CFLAGS)
LDFLAGS := -shared
LIBS    := $(GLIB_LIBS)

.PHONY: all clean

all: libhybrid_handoff.so

libhybrid_handoff.so: hybrid_handoff.c \
                     ../include/hybrid/state_abi.h \
                     ../include/hybrid/state_abi_check.h \
                     ../include/hybrid/insn_match.h
	@if [ ! -f $(QEMU_ROOT)/include/qemu/qemu-plugin.h ]; then \
	    echo "error: qemu-plugin.h not under $(QEMU_ROOT)/include/qemu/"; \
	    echo "       set QEMU_ROOT=<path> on the make command line"; \
	    exit 1; \
	fi
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) $(INCLUDES) $(LDFLAGS) -o $@ $< $(LIBS)

clean:
	rm -f libhybrid_handoff.so

input.new_string:
## Host dependencies

- C/C++ toolchain (clang preferred), CMake >= 3.20, ninja
- Python 3
- glib-2.0 dev (for the QEMU plugin)
- pkg-config
- Verilator (auto-detected; set `VSIM_VERILATOR_EXECUTABLE` if needed)
- libraries QEMU itself needs (pixman, slirp, etc. - see `hybrid_qemu/README.rst`)

`./build.sh qemu` configures QEMU as
`--target-list=riscv64-softmmu,riscv64-linux-user --disable-werror --disable-libnfs`.
Plugins are on by default in QEMU 9.x. `--disable-libnfs` avoids a known
host-package mismatch; `riscv64-linux-user` is included alongside softmmu
so the same QEMU build can run user-mode RV64 binaries when needed.

# hybrid_sim

Workspace for the Andes hybrid simulator: QEMU 9.x (functional, MHz) + vsim
(SystemC + Verilator, cycle-accurate) sharing memory and architectural state
across a magic-CSR boundary.

The driver itself - wire ABI, QEMU TCG plugin, RV64 fixtures, e2e harness -
lives at this top level. Two submodules provide the underlying simulators:

- `hybrid_qemu/` - QEMU fork (built outside CMake; autotools/meson)
- `hybrid_vsim/` - vsim sources (built via the top-level CMake project)

## Quickstart

```sh
git clone --recursive https://gitea.andestech.com/nick/hybrid_sim
cd hybrid_sim
./build.sh           # qemu, then top-level cmake --build (vsim + plugin + fixtures)
./build.sh test      # ctest -L hybrid-e2e (under build/)
```

Subcommands: `qemu`, `vsim`, `plugin`, `fixtures`, `test`, `clean`. Run
`./build.sh -h` for the full list.

A direct CMake invocation is equivalent for everything except QEMU:

```sh
./build.sh qemu                           # bootstraps QEMU once
cmake -S . -B build -GNinja
cmake --build build -j$(nproc)
ctest --test-dir build -L hybrid-e2e
```

## Host dependencies

- C/C++ toolchain (clang preferred), CMake >= 3.20, ninja
- Python 3
- glib-2.0 dev (for the QEMU plugin)
- pkg-config
- Verilator (auto-detected; set `VSIM_VERILATOR_EXECUTABLE` if needed)
- libraries QEMU itself needs (pixman, slirp, etc. - see `hybrid_qemu/README.rst`)

## Layout

| Path | Role |
|------|------|
| `include/hybrid/` | wire-ABI headers shared by QEMU plugin (C) and vsim (C++) |
| `qemu_plugin/` | `libhybrid_handoff.so` source + Makefile (links QEMU + glib) |
| `fixtures/` | bare-metal RV64 e2e fixtures (`.S`, `.ld`, Makefile) |
| `tests/` | end-to-end shell drivers (`roundtrip_e2e.sh`, `rt_*.sh`) |
| `cmake/` | top-level `Hybrid{Plugin,Fixtures,E2E}.cmake` |
| `hybrid_qemu/` | QEMU fork (submodule) |
| `hybrid_vsim/` | vsim simulator (submodule); `src/hybrid/` is its hybrid integration adapter |

## Outputs

After `./build.sh`:

- `hybrid_qemu/build/qemu-system-riscv64`
- `build/hybrid_vsim/sim_<cpu>` (e.g. `sim_ax45mpv_premium`)
- `qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so`
- `fixtures/*.elf`

lines: - libraries QEMU itself needs (pixman, slirp, etc. - see `hybrid_qemu/README.rst`)
+Plugins are on by default in QEMU 9.x. `--disable-libnfs` avoids a known
+so the same QEMU build can run user-mode RV64 binaries when needed.

input.old_string:
## Conventions specific to this repo

- **Hybrid is baked into vsim's main.cpp** (not gated by `VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID`). vsim therefore requires `HYBRID_DRIVER_DIR` to be set to a directory containing `include/hybrid/state_abi.h`. The top-level CMake project does this automatically (`add_subdirectory(hybrid_vsim)` forwards `HYBRID_DRIVER_DIR=${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}`). Standalone `cmake -S hybrid_vsim ...` invocations need `-DHYBRID_DRIVER_DIR=/path/to/hybrid_sim` explicitly.
input.new_string:
## Conventions specific to this repo

- **QEMU configure flags are pinned**: `build.sh qemu` runs `../configure --target-list=riscv64-softmmu,riscv64-linux-user --disable-werror --disable-libnfs`. Plugins default to ON in QEMU 9.x (no `--enable-plugins` needed). `--disable-libnfs` works around a host-package mismatch that breaks the configure step; do not remove it without retesting on the build host. `riscv64-linux-user` is bundled with softmmu so the same binary can run user-mode RV64 ELFs.
- **Hybrid is baked into vsim's main.cpp** (not gated by `VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID`). vsim therefore requires `HYBRID_DRIVER_DIR` to be set to a directory containing `include/hybrid/state_abi.h`. The top-level CMake project does this automatically (`add_subdirectory(hybrid_vsim)` forwards `HYBRID_DRIVER_DIR=${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}`). Standalone `cmake -S hybrid_vsim ...` invocations need `-DHYBRID_DRIVER_DIR=/path/to/hybrid_sim` explicitly.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Repository purpose

Workspace for the **Andes hybrid simulator**: QEMU 9.x (functional, MHz) + vsim (SystemC + Verilator, cycle-accurate) sharing memory and architectural state across a magic-CSR boundary. The intent is to run boot/libc/warm-up in QEMU at MHz, then hand off to vsim for the cycle-accurate region of interest (ROI), with optional hand-back to QEMU.

This top-level repo **owns the hybrid co-sim driver itself** (the wire ABI, the QEMU plugin, the bare-metal fixtures, and the e2e harness). Two submodules pinned to branch `hybrid` provide the underlying simulators:

- `hybrid_qemu/` - QEMU 9.x fork; built outside CMake (autotools/meson). The TCG plugin in `qemu_plugin/` links against its headers.
- `hybrid_vsim/` - vsim source tree, built via the top-level CMake project (`add_subdirectory(hybrid_vsim)` from `CMakeLists.txt`). The vsim-internal hybrid integration adapter lives at `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/` and includes the wire-ABI headers from this workspace's `include/hybrid/` via the `HYBRID_DRIVER_DIR` cache variable. The detailed design doc is `hybrid_vsim/hybrid_plan.md` (read this for any non-trivial change to the handoff path).

## Common commands

`build.sh` is the convenience entry point. Internally everything except QEMU goes through the top-level CMake project:

```sh
./build.sh            # qemu, then full top-level cmake --build (vsim + plugin + fixtures)
./build.sh qemu       # just QEMU (configure if needed, then ninja qemu-system-riscv64)
./build.sh vsim       # cmake --build (vsim binaries land under build/hybrid_vsim/)
./build.sh plugin     # cmake --build hybrid_handoff_plugin
./build.sh fixtures   # cmake --build hybrid_fixtures
./build.sh test       # ctest --test-dir build -L hybrid-e2e
./build.sh clean      # rm -rf hybrid_qemu/build, build/, plugin .so, fixture artifacts
```

Equivalent direct CMake invocation (after one-time `./build.sh qemu`):

```sh
cmake -S . -B build -GNinja
cmake --build build -j$(nproc)
ctest --test-dir build -L hybrid-e2e --output-on-failure
```

Run a single e2e fixture: `ctest --test-dir build -R test_e2e_rt_all_gprs --output-on-failure`.

Iterate on fixtures or plugin without re-running cmake: `make -C fixtures` and `make -C qemu_plugin QEMU_ROOT=$PWD/hybrid_qemu`.

## Architecture: how a hybrid run flows

A round-trip exercises three processes sharing a single packed `struct hybrid_state_v1` (defined in `include/hybrid/state_abi.h`, with `static_assert` offsets in `state_abi_check.h`):

1. **QEMU 1** runs with `qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so` loaded via `-plugin`. The plugin matches `csrrw rd,0x7C0,rs1` (mask `0xfff03073`); on `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` it drains GPR/FP/CSR via `qemu_plugin_read_register()`, writes the ABI struct to `outfile=`, and calls `qemu_plugin_request_exit()`.
2. **vsim** starts with `--resume-from-qemu <state.bin>`. Resume is driven **in-process** through the existing JTAG/DTM (`hybrid_vsim/src/platform/jtag_dtm.hpp`): halt -> Abstract Commands write x1..x31 + DPC -> `dret`. No DPI shim, no OpenOCD socket. vsim then runs cycle-accurately until a retire of a pre-scanned `csrw 0x7C0, 1` PC fires `CsrTrigger`, drains state again via DM, writes `--handoff-out`, and exits with `EXIT_HANDOFF_TO_QEMU = 200`.
3. **vsim's main** (on exit-200, when `--qemu-binary` is set) spawns **QEMU 2** with `-gdb -S -semihosting`. `qemu_handback.hpp` connects to the gdbstub, restores GPRs+PC, sends `c`, and the program completes via the RISC-V semihosting `ebreak`-triple.

Memory is **shared, not copied**: when `--shared-mem-path` is given, vsim's `SimpleMemory` is backed by an mmap of `/dev/shm/...` and QEMU is launched with `-object memory-backend-file,share=on,mem-path=...`. To make the file offsets agree, vsim's RAM region is **narrowed** in hybrid mode to `[0x80000000, 0x80000000+N)` (matching QEMU virt's `VIRT_DRAM`) instead of the default `[0x0, 0x8FFFFFFF]`. See `hybrid_plan.md` Section 9 - touching `simple_memory.hpp` / `platform.hpp` / `mmap_backing.hpp` without re-reading that section will likely re-break the offset contract.

Microarchitectural state (TLB, caches, BPU, store buffer) is **discarded** at handoff; the existing `--kanata-loop-skip N` mechanism warms up before recording.

## Critical files (most changes touch these)

Top-level driver assets:

- `include/hybrid/{state_abi.h,state_abi_check.h,insn_match.h,reg_names.h}` - shared wire-ABI; one source of truth for the QEMU plugin and vsim.
- `qemu_plugin/{hybrid_handoff.c,Makefile}` - QEMU TCG plugin. Standalone Make build; `-I../include` resolves to `include/hybrid/`.
- `fixtures/*.S` + `handoff*.ld` + `Makefile` - bare-metal RV64 fixtures using the three-phase template (Phase A in QEMU 1 -> `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` -> Phase B in vsim -> `csrwi 0x7C0, 1` -> Phase C in QEMU 2 -> semihosting `SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED`). Pass/fail signal is the QEMU 2 exit code.
- `tests/{roundtrip_e2e,rt_*}.sh` + `qemu_silent_stub.py` - end-to-end shell drivers that orchestrate QEMU+vsim+QEMU. `REPO_ROOT=$SCRIPT_DIR/..` resolves to this workspace root; defaults look under `build/hybrid_vsim/sim_<cpu>`, `qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so`, `fixtures/<name>.elf`.
- `cmake/{HybridPlugin,HybridFixtures,HybridE2E}.cmake` - top-level CTest registrations and `add_custom_target` wrappers around the standalone Makefiles.

Vsim-internal integration (stays in the submodule):

- `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/`:
  - `csr_trigger.hpp` - retire-PC watcher; pre-scans the ELF for `csrw 0x7C0, *` PCs.
  - `handoff_controller.hpp` - decodes `csrrw` form, dispatches enter (rs1==0) vs runtime-exit (rs1==1).
  - `state_drain.hpp` / `resume_driver.hpp` - DM Abstract-Command read/write of GPRs+DPC.
  - `qemu_handback.hpp` - spawns QEMU 2; builds `-object memory-backend-file` argv when `shared_mem_path` is set.
  - `gdbstub_client.hpp` / `gdbstub_session.hpp` - in-process gdbstub used to push state into QEMU 2.
  - `resume_from_qemu.hpp`, `state_loader.hpp` - ABI-struct ingest on the vsim side.
  - `exit_codes.hpp` - `EXIT_HANDOFF_TO_QEMU = 200` and friends.
- `hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` - registers vsim-linked hybrid unit tests; reads `${HYBRID_DRIVER_DIR}/include` for the wire-ABI include path.

## Conventions specific to this repo

- **Hybrid is baked into vsim's main.cpp** (not gated by `VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID`). vsim therefore requires `HYBRID_DRIVER_DIR` to be set to a directory containing `include/hybrid/state_abi.h`. The top-level CMake project does this automatically (`add_subdirectory(hybrid_vsim)` forwards `HYBRID_DRIVER_DIR=${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}`). Standalone `cmake -S hybrid_vsim ...` invocations need `-DHYBRID_DRIVER_DIR=/path/to/hybrid_sim` explicitly.
- **Submodules pinned to `hybrid` branch**: never push submodule changes from this superproject without first pushing the corresponding branch in the submodule's own remote.
- **Three-phase fixture template**: every new `rt_*.S` fixture must encode pass/fail into a GPR before `csrwi 0x7C0, 1`, then verify in Phase C and ship the result through semihosting `SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED`. FP/CSR/V state cannot be used as test channels - they are not yet round-tripped (Phase 5 work).
- **Hybrid state ABI is versioned**: `state_abi.h` carries magic `'AHYB'` + version; offsets are `static_assert`-checked in `state_abi_check.h`. Any layout change requires bumping the version and updating both the C plugin and C++ loaders in the same commit.
- **What is intentionally out of scope inside a ROI**: MMIO, lr/sc reservations across handoff, self-modifying code, and `mcycle`/`minstret` continuity. Don't add fallback paths for these without a Phase-5 plan.

lines: +- **QEMU configure flags are pinned**: `build.sh qemu` runs `../configure --target-list=riscv64-softmmu,riscv64-linux-user --disable-werror --disable-libnfs`. Plugins default to ON in QEMU 9.x (no `--enable-plugins` needed). `--disable-libnfs` works around a host-package mismatch that breaks the configure step; do not remove it without retesting on the build host. `riscv64-linux-user` is bundled with softmmu so the same binary can run user-mode RV64 ELFs.

- **Hybrid is baked into vsim's main.cpp** (not gated by `VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID`). vsim therefore requires `HYBRID_DRIVER_DIR` to be set to a directory containing `include/hybrid/state_abi.h`. The top-level CMake project does this automatically (`add_subdirectory(hybrid_vsim)` forwards `HYBRID_DRIVER_DIR=${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}`). Standalone `cmake -S hybrid_vsim ...` invocations need `-DHYBRID_DRIVER_DIR=/path/to/hybrid_sim` explicitly.
- **Submodules pinned to `hybrid` branch**: never push submodule changes from this superproject without first pushing the corresponding branch in the submodule's own remote.

- **Three-phase fixture template**: every new `rt_*.S` fixture must encode pass/fail into a GPR before `csrwi 0x7C0, 1`, then verify in Phase C and ship the result through semihosting `SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED`. FP/CSR/V state cannot be used as test channels - they are not yet round-tripped (Phase 5 work).

file.content:
# verilator must be >= v5.026
FROM docker.io/verilator/verilator:v5.046 AS verilator


# Base environment with development tools
FROM ubuntu:24.04 AS env

WORKDIR /work

RUN \
  apt-get update && \
  DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive apt-get install --yes --no-install-recommends \
  autoconf autoconf-archive automake autotools-dev bison ca-certificates clang \
  cmake curl flex g++ git help2man libboost-date-time-dev libboost-dev \
  libboost-filesystem-dev libboost-system-dev libfl-dev libfl2 libreadline-dev \
  libtool lld m4 make ninja-build patch perl perl-doc pkg-config vim \
  python3-dev python3-pybind11 ccache tclsh xz-utils zlib1g zlib1g-dev \
  python3-yaml && \
  apt-get clean && \
  rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

COPY --from=verilator /usr/local/share/verilator /usr/local/share/verilator
COPY --from=verilator /usr/local/bin /usr/local/bin


# Common build preparation
FROM env AS build-prep

COPY ./CMakeLists.txt ./config.yaml /work/
COPY ./cmake/ /work/cmake/
COPY ./data/ /work/data/
COPY ./external/ /work/external/
COPY ./src/ /work/src/
COPY ./tools/ /work/tools/
COPY ./stubs/ /work/stubs/


# Builds vsim from source (release mode)
FROM build-prep AS build

ARG VERSION=""
RUN \
  cmake -B build -G Ninja \
  -DCMAKE_C_COMPILER=clang \
  -DCMAKE_CXX_COMPILER=clang++ \
  -DCMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX=/work/vsim \
  -DVSIM_LINK_STATIC=ON \
  -DVSIM_VERSION_OVERRIDE=${VERSION} && \
  cmake --build build --target all && \
  cmake --install build

# Remove RTL, verilated objects and cpp, strip binaries
RUN \
  find build/V*/obj_sc -type f \( -name "*.v" -o -name "*.sv" -o -name "*.xml" -o -name "*.c" -o -name "*.cpp" -o -name "*.o" -o -name "*.d" \) -delete && \
  find build/V* -maxdepth 1 -mindepth 1 ! -name "obj_sc" -exec rm -rf {} + && \
  find /work/vsim/bin/ -type f -exec strip {} \;

# Reconfigure without verilator so the dev stage can relink without re-verilating
RUN \
  cmake -B build -G Ninja \
  -DVSIM_VERSION_OVERRIDE=${VERSION} \
  -DVSIM_NO_VERILATOR=ON


# Builds vsim from source with debug mode enabled
FROM build-prep AS debug

ARG VERSION=""
RUN \
  cmake -B build -G Ninja \
  -DCMAKE_C_COMPILER=clang \
  -DCMAKE_CXX_COMPILER=clang++ \
  -DCMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX=/work/vsim \
  -DVSIM_LINK_STATIC=ON \
  -DVSIM_DEBUG=ON \
  -DVSIM_VERSION_OVERRIDE=${VERSION} && \
  cmake --build build --target all && \
  cmake --install build


# Customer development environment, reveal platform source code but not RTL
FROM env AS dev

WORKDIR /work

COPY --from=build /work/src/ /work/src/
COPY --from=build /work/tools/ /work/tools/
# readonly config
COPY --from=build --chmod=444 /work/data/ /work/data/
COPY --from=build /work/build/ /work/build/
COPY --from=build /work/vsim/ /work/vsim/
# cmake build infrastructure (for rebuilding in dev)
COPY --from=build /work/CMakeLists.txt /work/config.yaml /work/
COPY --from=build /work/cmake/ /work/cmake/

CMD ["/bin/bash"]


# Binary only
FROM scratch
COPY --from=build /work/vsim /vsim

cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.20)
project(hybrid_sim NONE)

# The hybrid_sim workspace owns the wire ABI (include/hybrid/), the QEMU TCG
# plugin (qemu_plugin/), the bare-metal RV64 fixtures (fixtures/), and the
# end-to-end shell drivers (tests/). It pulls in hybrid_vsim as a CMake
# subproject and forwards HYBRID_DRIVER_DIR so vsim resolves the wire-ABI
# headers from this workspace's include/ instead of its own (now-removed)
# in-tree copy.
#
# QEMU is built outside CMake (autotools/meson) via build.sh's `qemu`
# subcommand. We do not subsume that build here.

include(GNUInstallDirs)
enable_testing()

# Single source of truth for hybrid co-sim wire artifacts. hybrid_vsim's
# CMakeLists.txt reads this cache var and uses ${HYBRID_DRIVER_DIR}/include
# as the include path for code that pulls in hybrid/state_abi.h etc.
set(HYBRID_DRIVER_DIR "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}" CACHE PATH
    "Path to the hybrid_sim workspace root (provides include/hybrid/, qemu_plugin/, fixtures/, tests/)")

# Forward the QEMU root and turn on hybrid features so vsim's CMake registers
# its hybrid unit tests against this workspace.
set(VSIM_QEMU_ROOT "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/hybrid_qemu" CACHE PATH "" FORCE)
set(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID ON CACHE BOOL "" FORCE)

add_subdirectory(hybrid_vsim)

include(cmake/HybridFixtures.cmake)
include(cmake/HybridPlugin.cmake)
include(cmake/HybridE2E.cmake)

cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.20)
project(hybrid_sim NONE)

# Top-level CMake project for the hybrid co-simulator workspace.
#
# Scope split (driven by where each part has to run):
#   vsim     - this CMakeLists, via add_subdirectory(hybrid_vsim). Built
#              inside the vsim-env container (Verilator, clang, etc.).
#   plugin   - hybrid_sim/qemu_plugin/Makefile, host-side. Needs glib +
#              hybrid_qemu/ headers; the vsim-env image carries neither.
#   fixtures - hybrid_sim/fixtures/Makefile, host-side. Needs an RV64 newlib
#              toolchain at the path baked into that Makefile.
#   QEMU     - hybrid_qemu/configure + ninja, host-side (autotools/meson).
#
# Plugin and fixtures are intentionally NOT pulled into this CMake project.
# Driving them via add_custom_target(... ALL ...) would force the container
# build to try to invoke their Makefiles too, which fails on missing host
# packages. build.sh wraps the host-side Makefiles directly.

include(GNUInstallDirs)
enable_testing()

# Single source of truth for hybrid co-sim wire artifacts. hybrid_vsim's
# CMakeLists.txt reads this cache var and uses ${HYBRID_DRIVER_DIR}/include
# as the include path for code that pulls in hybrid/state_abi.h etc.
set(HYBRID_DRIVER_DIR "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}" CACHE PATH
    "Path to the hybrid_sim workspace root (provides include/hybrid/, qemu_plugin/, fixtures/, tests/)")

# Forward the QEMU root and turn on hybrid features so vsim's CMake registers
# its hybrid unit tests against this workspace.
set(VSIM_QEMU_ROOT "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/hybrid_qemu" CACHE PATH "" FORCE)
set(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID ON CACHE BOOL "" FORCE)

add_subdirectory(hybrid_vsim)

include(cmake/HybridE2E.cmake)

cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.20)
project(hybrid_sim NONE)

# The hybrid_sim workspace owns the wire ABI (include/hybrid/), the QEMU TCG
# plugin (qemu_plugin/), the bare-metal RV64 fixtures (fixtures/), and the
# end-to-end shell drivers (tests/). It pulls in hybrid_vsim as a CMake
# subproject and forwards HYBRID_DRIVER_DIR so vsim resolves the wire-ABI
# headers from this workspace's include/ instead of its own (now-removed)
# in-tree copy.
#
# QEMU is built outside CMake (autotools/meson) via build.sh's `qemu`
# subcommand. We do not subsume that build here.

include(GNUInstallDirs)
enable_testing()

# Single source of truth for hybrid co-sim wire artifacts. hybrid_vsim's
# CMakeLists.txt reads this cache var and uses ${HYBRID_DRIVER_DIR}/include
# as the include path for code that pulls in hybrid/state_abi.h etc.
set(HYBRID_DRIVER_DIR "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}" CACHE PATH
    "Path to the hybrid_sim workspace root (provides include/hybrid/, qemu_plugin/, fixtures/, tests/)")

# Forward the QEMU root and turn on hybrid features so vsim's CMake registers
# its hybrid unit tests against this workspace.
set(VSIM_QEMU_ROOT "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/hybrid_qemu" CACHE PATH "" FORCE)
set(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID ON CACHE BOOL "" FORCE)

add_subdirectory(hybrid_vsim)

include(cmake/HybridFixtures.cmake)
include(cmake/HybridPlugin.cmake)
include(cmake/HybridE2E.cmake)

content.text: Now rewrite `build.sh` so `vsim` runs inside the `vsim-env` container while plugin and fixtures stay on the host.

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Orchestrates the hybrid simulator build.
#
# Where each part runs:
#   qemu     - host (hybrid_qemu autotools/meson)
#   vsim     - INSIDE the vsim-env container (Verilator + clang). Mirrors
#              option 3 in hybrid_vsim/README.md. Image is built on demand.
#   plugin   - host (qemu_plugin/Makefile; needs glib-2.0 + hybrid_qemu headers)
#   fixtures - host (fixtures/Makefile; needs the Andes RV64 newlib toolchain)
#   test     - host (ctest drives bash drivers under tests/)

set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
QEMU_DIR="$ROOT/hybrid_qemu"
QEMU_BUILD="$QEMU_DIR/build"
VSIM_SRC="$ROOT/hybrid_vsim"
PLUGIN_DIR="$ROOT/qemu_plugin"
FIXTURES_DIR="$ROOT/fixtures"
BUILD_DIR="$ROOT/build"

JOBS="$(nproc)"

DOCKER="${DOCKER:-docker}"
VSIM_DOCKER_IMAGE="${VSIM_DOCKER_IMAGE:-vsim-env:latest}"

c_step()  { printf '\033[1;34m==>\033[0m \033[1m%s\033[0m\n' "$*"; }
c_ok()    { printf '\033[1;32m[ok]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
c_err()   { printf '\033[1;31m[err]\033[0m %s\n' "$*" >&2; }

require_dir() {
  if [[ ! -d "$1" ]]; then
    c_err "missing $1 - did you run \`git submodule update --init --recursive\`?"
    exit 1
  fi
}

build_qemu() {
  require_dir "$QEMU_DIR"
  c_step "QEMU: configure (if needed)"
  if [[ ! -f "$QEMU_BUILD/build.ninja" && ! -f "$QEMU_BUILD/Makefile" ]]; then
    mkdir -p "$QEMU_BUILD"
    (cd "$QEMU_BUILD" && ../configure \
      --target-list=riscv64-softmmu,riscv64-linux-user \
      --disable-werror \
      --disable-libnfs)
  fi
  c_step "QEMU: build qemu-system-riscv64"
  if [[ -f "$QEMU_BUILD/build.ninja" ]]; then
    ninja -C "$QEMU_BUILD" qemu-system-riscv64
  else
    make -C "$QEMU_BUILD" -j"$JOBS" qemu-system-riscv64
  fi
  c_ok "QEMU built: $QEMU_BUILD/qemu-system-riscv64"
}

ensure_vsim_image() {
  if ! "$DOCKER" image inspect "$VSIM_DOCKER_IMAGE" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    c_step "vsim: build container image $VSIM_DOCKER_IMAGE (env stage)"
    "$DOCKER" build --target env -t "$VSIM_DOCKER_IMAGE" \
      -f "$VSIM_SRC/Dockerfile" "$VSIM_SRC"
  fi
}

run_in_vsim_container() {
  ensure_vsim_image
  "$DOCKER" run --rm \
    -v "$ROOT:/work" \
    -w /work \
    -u "$(id -u):$(id -g)" \
    "$VSIM_DOCKER_IMAGE" \
    bash -c "$1"
}

build_vsim() {
  require_dir "$VSIM_SRC"
  c_step "vsim: configure + build inside $VSIM_DOCKER_IMAGE"
  run_in_vsim_container \
    "cmake -S /work -B /work/build -GNinja \
       -DCMAKE_C_COMPILER=clang -DCMAKE_CXX_COMPILER=clang++ \
     && cmake --build /work/build -j${JOBS}"
  c_ok "vsim build artifacts under: $BUILD_DIR/hybrid_vsim/"
}

build_plugin() {
  require_dir "$PLUGIN_DIR"
  c_step "plugin: build libhybrid_handoff.so (host)"
  make -C "$PLUGIN_DIR" QEMU_ROOT="$QEMU_DIR"
  c_ok "plugin built: $PLUGIN_DIR/libhybrid_handoff.so"
}

build_fixtures() {
  require_dir "$FIXTURES_DIR"
  c_step "fixtures: build RV64 ELFs (host)"
  make -C "$FIXTURES_DIR"
  c_ok "fixtures built under: $FIXTURES_DIR/"
}

run_tests() {
  if [[ ! -f "$BUILD_DIR/CTestTestfile.cmake" ]]; then
    c_err "no CTest configuration in $BUILD_DIR; run \`./build.sh vsim\` first"
    exit 1
  fi
  c_step "ctest: hybrid-e2e label"
  ctest --test-dir "$BUILD_DIR" -L hybrid-e2e --output-on-failure
}

clean_all() {
  c_step "clean: remove build artifacts"
  rm -rf "$QEMU_BUILD" "$BUILD_DIR"
  rm -f  "$PLUGIN_DIR/libhybrid_handoff.so"
  make -C "$FIXTURES_DIR" clean >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
  c_ok "cleaned"
}

usage() {
  cat <<EOF
usage: $(basename "$0") [qemu|vsim|plugin|fixtures|test|clean|all]

  (no args)  same as 'all'
  qemu       configure + build hybrid_qemu/build/qemu-system-riscv64 (host)
  vsim       cmake configure + build inside the vsim-env container
             (auto-builds vsim-env:latest from hybrid_vsim/Dockerfile if missing).
             Override the image name with VSIM_DOCKER_IMAGE; swap engines via DOCKER=podman.
  plugin     make -C qemu_plugin QEMU_ROOT=hybrid_qemu (host; needs glib-2.0)
  fixtures   make -C fixtures (host; needs the Andes RV64 newlib toolchain)
  test       ctest --test-dir build -L hybrid-e2e (host)
  clean      remove qemu/build, top-level build/, plugin .so, fixture artifacts
  all        qemu, then vsim (container), then plugin + fixtures (host)
EOF
}

cmd="${1:-all}"
case "$cmd" in
  qemu)     build_qemu ;;
  vsim)     build_vsim ;;
  plugin)   build_plugin ;;
  fixtures) build_fixtures ;;
  test)     run_tests ;;
  clean)    clean_all ;;
  all)      build_qemu; build_vsim; build_plugin; build_fixtures ;;
  -h|--help|help) usage ;;
  *)        c_err "unknown command: $cmd"; usage; exit 2 ;;
esac

  if [[ ! -f "$QEMU_BUILD/build.ninja" && ! -f "$QEMU_BUILD/Makefile" ]]; then
    mkdir -p "$QEMU_BUILD"
    (cd "$QEMU_BUILD" && ../configure \
      --target-list=riscv64-softmmu,riscv64-linux-user \
      --disable-werror \
      --disable-libnfs)
  fi
  c_step "QEMU: build qemu-system-riscv64"
  if [[ -f "$QEMU_BUILD/build.ninja" ]]; then
    ninja -C "$QEMU_BUILD" qemu-system-riscv64
  else
    make -C "$QEMU_BUILD" -j"$JOBS" qemu-system-riscv64
  fi
  c_ok "QEMU built: $QEMU_BUILD/qemu-system-riscv64"
}

ensure_vsim_image() {
  if ! "$DOCKER" image inspect "$VSIM_DOCKER_IMAGE" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    c_step "vsim: build container image $VSIM_DOCKER_IMAGE (env stage)"
    "$DOCKER" build --target env -t "$VSIM_DOCKER_IMAGE" \
      -f "$VSIM_SRC/Dockerfile" "$VSIM_SRC"
  fi
}

run_in_vsim_container() {
  ensure_vsim_image
  "$DOCKER" run --rm \
    -v "$ROOT:/work" \
    -w /work \
    -u "$(id -u):$(id -g)" \
    "$VSIM_DOCKER_IMAGE" \
    bash -c "$1"
}

build_vsim() {
  require_dir "$VSIM_SRC"
  c_step "vsim: configure + build inside $VSIM_DOCKER_IMAGE"
  run_in_vsim_container \
    "cmake -S /work -B /work/build -GNinja \
       -DCMAKE_C_COMPILER=clang -DCMAKE_CXX_COMPILER=clang++ \
     && cmake --build /work/build -j${JOBS}"
  c_ok "vsim build artifacts under: $BUILD_DIR/hybrid_vsim/"
}

build_plugin() {
  require_dir "$PLUGIN_DIR"
  c_step "plugin: build libhybrid_handoff.so (host)"
  make -C "$PLUGIN_DIR" QEMU_ROOT="$QEMU_DIR"
  c_ok "plugin built: $PLUGIN_DIR/libhybrid_handoff.so"
}

build_fixtures() {
  require_dir "$FIXTURES_DIR"
  c_step "fixtures: build RV64 ELFs (host)"
  make -C "$FIXTURES_DIR"
  c_ok "fixtures built under: $FIXTURES_DIR/"
}

run_tests() {
  if [[ ! -f "$BUILD_DIR/CTestTestfile.cmake" ]]; then
    c_err "no CTest configuration in $BUILD_DIR; run \`./build.sh vsim\` first"
    exit 1
  fi
  c_step "ctest: hybrid-e2e label"
  ctest --test-dir "$BUILD_DIR" -L hybrid-e2e --output-on-failure
}

clean_all() {
  c_step "clean: remove build artifacts"
  rm -rf "$QEMU_BUILD" "$BUILD_DIR"
  rm -f  "$PLUGIN_DIR/libhybrid_handoff.so"
  make -C "$FIXTURES_DIR" clean >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
  c_ok "cleaned"
}

usage() {
  cat <<EOF
usage: $(basename "$0") [qemu|vsim|plugin|fixtures|test|clean|all]

  (no args)  same as 'all'
  qemu       configure + build hybrid_qemu/build/qemu-system-riscv64 (host)
  vsim       cmake configure + build inside the vsim-env container
             (auto-builds vsim-env:latest from hybrid_vsim/Dockerfile if missing).
             Override the image name with VSIM_DOCKER_IMAGE; swap engines via DOCKER=podman.
  plugin     make -C qemu_plugin QEMU_ROOT=hybrid_qemu (host; needs glib-2.0)
  fixtures   make -C fixtures (host; needs the Andes RV64 newlib toolchain)
  test       ctest --test-dir build -L hybrid-e2e (host)
  clean      remove qemu/build, top-level build/, plugin .so, fixture artifacts
  all        qemu, then vsim (container), then plugin + fixtures (host)
EOF
}

cmd="${1:-all}"
case "$cmd" in
  qemu)     build_qemu ;;
  vsim)     build_vsim ;;
  plugin)   build_plugin ;;
  fixtures) build_fixtures ;;
  test)     run_tests ;;
  clean)    clean_all ;;
  all)      build_qemu; build_vsim; build_plugin; build_fixtures ;;
  -h|--help|help) usage ;;
  *)        c_err "unknown command: $cmd"; usage; exit 2 ;;
esac

input.old_string:
## Quickstart

```sh
git clone --recursive https://gitea.andestech.com/nick/hybrid_sim
cd hybrid_sim
./build.sh           # qemu, then top-level cmake --build (vsim + plugin + fixtures)
./build.sh test      # ctest -L hybrid-e2e (under build/)
```

Subcommands: `qemu`, `vsim`, `plugin`, `fixtures`, `test`, `clean`. Run
`./build.sh -h` for the full list.

A direct CMake invocation is equivalent for everything except QEMU:

```sh
./build.sh qemu                           # bootstraps QEMU once
cmake -S . -B build -GNinja
cmake --build build -j$(nproc)
ctest --test-dir build -L hybrid-e2e
```

## Host dependencies

- C/C++ toolchain (clang preferred), CMake >= 3.20, ninja
- Python 3
- glib-2.0 dev (for the QEMU plugin)
- pkg-config
- Verilator (auto-detected; set `VSIM_VERILATOR_EXECUTABLE` if needed)
- libraries QEMU itself needs (pixman, slirp, etc. - see `hybrid_qemu/README.rst`)

`./build.sh qemu` configures QEMU as
`--target-list=riscv64-softmmu,riscv64-linux-user --disable-werror --disable-libnfs`.
Plugins are on by default in QEMU 9.x. `--disable-libnfs` avoids a known
host-package mismatch; `riscv64-linux-user` is included alongside softmmu
so the same QEMU build can run user-mode RV64 binaries when needed.
input.new_string:
## Quickstart

```sh
git clone --recursive https://gitea.andestech.com/nick/hybrid_sim
cd hybrid_sim
./build.sh           # qemu (host), vsim (container), plugin + fixtures (host)
./build.sh test      # ctest -L hybrid-e2e (under build/)
```

Subcommands: `qemu`, `vsim`, `plugin`, `fixtures`, `test`, `clean`. Run
`./build.sh -h` for the full list.

## Where each step runs

| Step | Where | Why |
|---|---|---|
| `qemu` | host (autotools/meson) | QEMU build is independent and uses host system libs |
| `vsim` | inside `vsim-env:latest` container | needs Verilator + clang + boost + a long apt list; mirrors `hybrid_vsim/README.md` option 3. The first invocation builds the image from `hybrid_vsim/Dockerfile` (`--target env`). |
| `plugin` | host | `qemu_plugin/Makefile` needs glib-2.0 + `hybrid_qemu/include/qemu/*.h`; the vsim-env image carries neither. |
| `fixtures` | host | `fixtures/Makefile` needs the Andes RV64 newlib toolchain at the path baked into that Makefile. |
| `test` | host | `ctest` drives the bash drivers under `tests/`, which `exec` the host-runnable simulator binaries. |

Override the engine or image via env vars:

```sh
DOCKER=podman ./build.sh vsim                          # swap docker -> podman
VSIM_DOCKER_IMAGE=my-vsim-env:dev ./build.sh vsim      # use a pre-built image
```

## Host dependencies

- glib-2.0 dev + pkg-config (for the QEMU plugin)
- libraries QEMU itself needs (pixman, slirp, etc. - see `hybrid_qemu/README.rst`)
- Andes RV64 newlib toolchain (default path baked into `fixtures/Makefile`)
- docker or podman, Python 3, and CMake >= 3.20 on the host driving `build.sh`
- vsim's compile-time dependencies (Verilator, clang, boost, ...) are NOT
  needed on the host - they live inside the `vsim-env` container

`./build.sh qemu` configures QEMU as
`--target-list=riscv64-softmmu,riscv64-linux-user --disable-werror --disable-libnfs`.
Plugins are on by default in QEMU 9.x. `--disable-libnfs` avoids a known
host-package mismatch; `riscv64-linux-user` is included alongside softmmu
so the same QEMU build can run user-mode RV64 binaries when needed.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# hybrid_sim

Workspace for the Andes hybrid simulator: QEMU 9.x (functional, MHz) + vsim
(SystemC + Verilator, cycle-accurate) sharing memory and architectural state
across a magic-CSR boundary.

The driver itself - wire ABI, QEMU TCG plugin, RV64 fixtures, e2e harness -
lives at this top level. Two submodules provide the underlying simulators:

- `hybrid_qemu/` - QEMU fork (built outside CMake; autotools/meson)
- `hybrid_vsim/` - vsim sources (built via the top-level CMake project)

## Quickstart

```sh
git clone --recursive https://gitea.andestech.com/nick/hybrid_sim
cd hybrid_sim
./build.sh           # qemu, then top-level cmake --build (vsim + plugin + fixtures)
./build.sh test      # ctest -L hybrid-e2e (under build/)
```

Subcommands: `qemu`, `vsim`, `plugin`, `fixtures`, `test`, `clean`. Run
`./build.sh -h` for the full list.

A direct CMake invocation is equivalent for everything except QEMU:

```sh
./build.sh qemu                           # bootstraps QEMU once
cmake -S . -B build -GNinja
cmake --build build -j$(nproc)
ctest --test-dir build -L hybrid-e2e
```

## Host dependencies

- C/C++ toolchain (clang preferred), CMake >= 3.20, ninja
- Python 3
- glib-2.0 dev (for the QEMU plugin)
- pkg-config
- Verilator (auto-detected; set `VSIM_VERILATOR_EXECUTABLE` if needed)
- libraries QEMU itself needs (pixman, slirp, etc. - see `hybrid_qemu/README.rst`)

`./build.sh qemu` configures QEMU as
`--target-list=riscv64-softmmu,riscv64-linux-user --disable-werror --disable-libnfs`.
Plugins are on by default in QEMU 9.x. `--disable-libnfs` avoids a known
host-package mismatch; `riscv64-linux-user` is included alongside softmmu
so the same QEMU build can run user-mode RV64 binaries when needed.

## Layout

| Path | Role |
|------|------|
| `include/hybrid/` | wire-ABI headers shared by QEMU plugin (C) and vsim (C++) |
| `qemu_plugin/` | `libhybrid_handoff.so` source + Makefile (links QEMU + glib) |
| `fixtures/` | bare-metal RV64 e2e fixtures (`.S`, `.ld`, Makefile) |
| `tests/` | end-to-end shell drivers (`roundtrip_e2e.sh`, `rt_*.sh`) |
| `cmake/` | top-level `Hybrid{Plugin,Fixtures,E2E}.cmake` |
| `hybrid_qemu/` | QEMU fork (submodule) |
| `hybrid_vsim/` | vsim simulator (submodule); `src/hybrid/` is its hybrid integration adapter |

## Outputs

After `./build.sh`:

- `hybrid_qemu/build/qemu-system-riscv64`
- `build/hybrid_vsim/sim_<cpu>` (e.g. `sim_ax45mpv_premium`)
- `qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so`
- `fixtures/*.elf`

input.old_string:
## Common commands

`build.sh` is the convenience entry point. Internally everything except QEMU goes through the top-level CMake project:

```sh
./build.sh            # qemu, then full top-level cmake --build (vsim + plugin + fixtures)
./build.sh qemu       # just QEMU (configure if needed, then ninja qemu-system-riscv64)
./build.sh vsim       # cmake --build (vsim binaries land under build/hybrid_vsim/)
./build.sh plugin     # cmake --build hybrid_handoff_plugin
./build.sh fixtures   # cmake --build hybrid_fixtures
./build.sh test       # ctest --test-dir build -L hybrid-e2e
./build.sh clean      # rm -rf hybrid_qemu/build, build/, plugin .so, fixture artifacts
```

Equivalent direct CMake invocation (after one-time `./build.sh qemu`):

```sh
cmake -S . -B build -GNinja
cmake --build build -j$(nproc)
ctest --test-dir build -L hybrid-e2e --output-on-failure
```

Run a single e2e fixture: `ctest --test-dir build -R test_e2e_rt_all_gprs --output-on-failure`.

Iterate on fixtures or plugin without re-running cmake: `make -C fixtures` and `make -C qemu_plugin QEMU_ROOT=$PWD/hybrid_qemu`.
input.new_string:
## Common commands

`build.sh` is the entry point. Each subcommand runs in the environment that part of the build needs, so do not assume a single `cmake --build` produces everything.

```sh
./build.sh             # qemu (host), vsim (container), plugin + fixtures (host)
./build.sh qemu        # host autotools/meson; produces hybrid_qemu/build/qemu-system-riscv64
./build.sh vsim        # cmake configure + build INSIDE the vsim-env container; binaries land in build/hybrid_vsim/
./build.sh plugin      # host: make -C qemu_plugin QEMU_ROOT=$PWD/hybrid_qemu
./build.sh fixtures    # host: make -C fixtures
./build.sh test        # host: ctest --test-dir build -L hybrid-e2e
./build.sh clean       # rm -rf hybrid_qemu/build, build/, plugin .so, fixture artifacts
```

`./build.sh vsim` auto-builds the `vsim-env:latest` container image from `hybrid_vsim/Dockerfile` (`--target env`) on first use. Override with `VSIM_DOCKER_IMAGE=...` or swap engines with `DOCKER=podman`.

The top-level `CMakeLists.txt` does **only** `add_subdirectory(hybrid_vsim)` plus `include(cmake/HybridE2E.cmake)`. Plugin and fixtures are intentionally NOT registered as cmake targets - their host-only Makefile dependencies (glib-2.0, RV64 newlib toolchain) are not present in the vsim-env container, so pulling them into the cmake project would break the container build.

Run a single e2e fixture (after a full build): `ctest --test-dir build -R test_e2e_rt_all_gprs --output-on-failure`.

Iterate on fixtures or plugin without re-running build.sh: `make -C fixtures` and `make -C qemu_plugin QEMU_ROOT=$PWD/hybrid_qemu`.

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Repository purpose

Workspace for the **Andes hybrid simulator**: QEMU 9.x (functional, MHz) + vsim (SystemC + Verilator, cycle-accurate) sharing memory and architectural state across a magic-CSR boundary. The intent is to run boot/libc/warm-up in QEMU at MHz, then hand off to vsim for the cycle-accurate region of interest (ROI), with optional hand-back to QEMU.

This top-level repo **owns the hybrid co-sim driver itself** (the wire ABI, the QEMU plugin, the bare-metal fixtures, and the e2e harness). Two submodules pinned to branch `hybrid` provide the underlying simulators:

- `hybrid_qemu/` - QEMU 9.x fork; built outside CMake (autotools/meson). The TCG plugin in `qemu_plugin/` links against its headers.
- `hybrid_vsim/` - vsim source tree, built via the top-level CMake project (`add_subdirectory(hybrid_vsim)` from `CMakeLists.txt`). The vsim-internal hybrid integration adapter lives at `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/` and includes the wire-ABI headers from this workspace's `include/hybrid/` via the `HYBRID_DRIVER_DIR` cache variable. The detailed design doc is `hybrid_vsim/hybrid_plan.md` (read this for any non-trivial change to the handoff path).

## Common commands

`build.sh` is the convenience entry point. Internally everything except QEMU goes through the top-level CMake project:

```sh
./build.sh            # qemu, then full top-level cmake --build (vsim + plugin + fixtures)
./build.sh qemu       # just QEMU (configure if needed, then ninja qemu-system-riscv64)
./build.sh vsim       # cmake --build (vsim binaries land under build/hybrid_vsim/)
./build.sh plugin     # cmake --build hybrid_handoff_plugin
./build.sh fixtures   # cmake --build hybrid_fixtures
./build.sh test       # ctest --test-dir build -L hybrid-e2e
./build.sh clean      # rm -rf hybrid_qemu/build, build/, plugin .so, fixture artifacts
```

Equivalent direct CMake invocation (after one-time `./build.sh qemu`):

```sh
cmake -S . -B build -GNinja
cmake --build build -j$(nproc)
ctest --test-dir build -L hybrid-e2e --output-on-failure
```

Run a single e2e fixture: `ctest --test-dir build -R test_e2e_rt_all_gprs --output-on-failure`.

Iterate on fixtures or plugin without re-running cmake: `make -C fixtures` and `make -C qemu_plugin QEMU_ROOT=$PWD/hybrid_qemu`.

## Architecture: how a hybrid run flows

A round-trip exercises three processes sharing a single packed `struct hybrid_state_v1` (defined in `include/hybrid/state_abi.h`, with `static_assert` offsets in `state_abi_check.h`):

1. **QEMU 1** runs with `qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so` loaded via `-plugin`. The plugin matches `csrrw rd,0x7C0,rs1` (mask `0xfff03073`); on `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` it drains GPR/FP/CSR via `qemu_plugin_read_register()`, writes the ABI struct to `outfile=`, and calls `qemu_plugin_request_exit()`.
2. **vsim** starts with `--resume-from-qemu <state.bin>`. Resume is driven **in-process** through the existing JTAG/DTM (`hybrid_vsim/src/platform/jtag_dtm.hpp`): halt -> Abstract Commands write x1..x31 + DPC -> `dret`. No DPI shim, no OpenOCD socket. vsim then runs cycle-accurately until a retire of a pre-scanned `csrw 0x7C0, 1` PC fires `CsrTrigger`, drains state again via DM, writes `--handoff-out`, and exits with `EXIT_HANDOFF_TO_QEMU = 200`.
3. **vsim's main** (on exit-200, when `--qemu-binary` is set) spawns **QEMU 2** with `-gdb -S -semihosting`. `qemu_handback.hpp` connects to the gdbstub, restores GPRs+PC, sends `c`, and the program completes via the RISC-V semihosting `ebreak`-triple.

Memory is **shared, not copied**: when `--shared-mem-path` is given, vsim's `SimpleMemory` is backed by an mmap of `/dev/shm/...` and QEMU is launched with `-object memory-backend-file,share=on,mem-path=...`. To make the file offsets agree, vsim's RAM region is **narrowed** in hybrid mode to `[0x80000000, 0x80000000+N)` (matching QEMU virt's `VIRT_DRAM`) instead of the default `[0x0, 0x8FFFFFFF]`. See `hybrid_plan.md` Section 9 - touching `simple_memory.hpp` / `platform.hpp` / `mmap_backing.hpp` without re-reading that section will likely re-break the offset contract.

Microarchitectural state (TLB, caches, BPU, store buffer) is **discarded** at handoff; the existing `--kanata-loop-skip N` mechanism warms up before recording.

## Critical files (most changes touch these)

Top-level driver assets:

- `include/hybrid/{state_abi.h,state_abi_check.h,insn_match.h,reg_names.h}` - shared wire-ABI; one source of truth for the QEMU plugin and vsim.
- `qemu_plugin/{hybrid_handoff.c,Makefile}` - QEMU TCG plugin. Standalone Make build; `-I../include` resolves to `include/hybrid/`.
- `fixtures/*.S` + `handoff*.ld` + `Makefile` - bare-metal RV64 fixtures using the three-phase template (Phase A in QEMU 1 -> `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` -> Phase B in vsim -> `csrwi 0x7C0, 1` -> Phase C in QEMU 2 -> semihosting `SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED`). Pass/fail signal is the QEMU 2 exit code.
- `tests/{roundtrip_e2e,rt_*}.sh` + `qemu_silent_stub.py` - end-to-end shell drivers that orchestrate QEMU+vsim+QEMU. `REPO_ROOT=$SCRIPT_DIR/..` resolves to this workspace root; defaults look under `build/hybrid_vsim/sim_<cpu>`, `qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so`, `fixtures/<name>.elf`.
- `cmake/{HybridPlugin,HybridFixtures,HybridE2E}.cmake` - top-level CTest registrations and `add_custom_target` wrappers around the standalone Makefiles.

Vsim-internal integration (stays in the submodule):

- `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/`:
  - `csr_trigger.hpp` - retire-PC watcher; pre-scans the ELF for `csrw 0x7C0, *` PCs.
  - `handoff_controller.hpp` - decodes `csrrw` form, dispatches enter (rs1==0) vs runtime-exit (rs1==1).
  - `state_drain.hpp` / `resume_driver.hpp` - DM Abstract-Command read/write of GPRs+DPC.
  - `qemu_handback.hpp` - spawns QEMU 2; builds `-object memory-backend-file` argv when `shared_mem_path` is set.
  - `gdbstub_client.hpp` / `gdbstub_session.hpp` - in-process gdbstub used to push state into QEMU 2.
  - `resume_from_qemu.hpp`, `state_loader.hpp` - ABI-struct ingest on the vsim side.
  - `exit_codes.hpp` - `EXIT_HANDOFF_TO_QEMU = 200` and friends.
- `hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` - registers vsim-linked hybrid unit tests; reads `${HYBRID_DRIVER_DIR}/include` for the wire-ABI include path.

## Conventions specific to this repo

- **QEMU configure flags are pinned**: `build.sh qemu` runs `../configure --target-list=riscv64-softmmu,riscv64-linux-user --disable-werror --disable-libnfs`. Plugins default to ON in QEMU 9.x (no `--enable-plugins` needed). `--disable-libnfs` works around a host-package mismatch that breaks the configure step; do not remove it without retesting on the build host. `riscv64-linux-user` is bundled with softmmu so the same binary can run user-mode RV64 ELFs.
- **Hybrid is baked into vsim's main.cpp** (not gated by `VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID`). vsim therefore requires `HYBRID_DRIVER_DIR` to be set to a directory containing `include/hybrid/state_abi.h`. The top-level CMake project does this automatically (`add_subdirectory(hybrid_vsim)` forwards `HYBRID_DRIVER_DIR=${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}`). Standalone `cmake -S hybrid_vsim ...` invocations need `-DHYBRID_DRIVER_DIR=/path/to/hybrid_sim` explicitly.
- **Submodules pinned to `hybrid` branch**: never push submodule changes from this superproject without first pushing the corresponding branch in the submodule's own remote.
- **Three-phase fixture template**: every new `rt_*.S` fixture must encode pass/fail into a GPR before `csrwi 0x7C0, 1`, then verify in Phase C and ship the result through semihosting `SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED`. FP/CSR/V state cannot be used as test channels - they are not yet round-tripped (Phase 5 work).
- **Hybrid state ABI is versioned**: `state_abi.h` carries magic `'AHYB'` + version; offsets are `static_assert`-checked in `state_abi_check.h`. Any layout change requires bumping the version and updating both the C plugin and C++ loaders in the same commit.
- **What is intentionally out of scope inside a ROI**: MMIO, lr/sc reservations across handoff, self-modifying code, and `mcycle`/`minstret` continuity. Don't add fallback paths for these without a Phase-5 plan.

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Repository purpose

Workspace for the **Andes hybrid simulator**: QEMU 9.x (functional, MHz) + vsim (SystemC + Verilator, cycle-accurate) sharing memory and architectural state across a magic-CSR boundary. The intent is to run boot/libc/warm-up in QEMU at MHz, then hand off to vsim for the cycle-accurate region of interest (ROI), with optional hand-back to QEMU.

This top-level repo **owns the hybrid co-sim driver itself** (the wire ABI, the QEMU plugin, the bare-metal fixtures, and the e2e harness). Two submodules pinned to branch `hybrid` provide the underlying simulators:

- `hybrid_qemu/` - QEMU 9.x fork; built outside CMake (autotools/meson). The TCG plugin in `qemu_plugin/` links against its headers.
- `hybrid_vsim/` - vsim source tree, built via the top-level CMake project (`add_subdirectory(hybrid_vsim)` from `CMakeLists.txt`). The vsim-internal hybrid integration adapter lives at `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/` and includes the wire-ABI headers from this workspace's `include/hybrid/` via the `HYBRID_DRIVER_DIR` cache variable. The detailed design doc is `hybrid_vsim/hybrid_plan.md` (read this for any non-trivial change to the handoff path).

## Common commands

`build.sh` is the entry point. Each subcommand runs in the environment that part of the build needs, so do not assume a single `cmake --build` produces everything.

```sh
./build.sh             # qemu (host), vsim (container), plugin + fixtures (host)
./build.sh qemu        # host autotools/meson; produces hybrid_qemu/build/qemu-system-riscv64
./build.sh vsim        # cmake configure + build INSIDE the vsim-env container; binaries land in build/hybrid_vsim/
./build.sh plugin      # host: make -C qemu_plugin QEMU_ROOT=$PWD/hybrid_qemu
./build.sh fixtures    # host: make -C fixtures
./build.sh test        # host: ctest --test-dir build -L hybrid-e2e
./build.sh clean       # rm -rf hybrid_qemu/build, build/, plugin .so, fixture artifacts
```

`./build.sh vsim` auto-builds the `vsim-env:latest` container image from `hybrid_vsim/Dockerfile` (`--target env`) on first use. Override with `VSIM_DOCKER_IMAGE=...` or swap engines with `DOCKER=podman`.

The top-level `CMakeLists.txt` does **only** `add_subdirectory(hybrid_vsim)` plus `include(cmake/HybridE2E.cmake)`. Plugin and fixtures are intentionally NOT registered as cmake targets - their host-only Makefile dependencies (glib-2.0, RV64 newlib toolchain) are not present in the vsim-env container, so pulling them into the cmake project would break the container build.

Run a single e2e fixture (after a full build): `ctest --test-dir build -R test_e2e_rt_all_gprs --output-on-failure`.

Iterate on fixtures or plugin without re-running build.sh: `make -C fixtures` and `make -C qemu_plugin QEMU_ROOT=$PWD/hybrid_qemu`.

2. **vsim** starts with `--resume-from-qemu <state.bin>`. Resume is driven **in-process** through the existing JTAG/DTM (`hybrid_vsim/src/platform/jtag_dtm.hpp`): halt -> Abstract Commands write x1..x31 + DPC -> `dret`. No DPI shim, no OpenOCD socket. vsim then runs cycle-accurately until a retire of a pre-scanned `csrw 0x7C0, 1` PC fires `CsrTrigger`, drains state again via DM, writes `--handoff-out`, and exits with `EXIT_HANDOFF_TO_QEMU = 200`.
3. **vsim's main** (on exit-200, when `--qemu-binary` is set) spawns **QEMU 2** with `-gdb -S -semihosting`. `qemu_handback.hpp` connects to the gdbstub, restores GPRs+PC, sends `c`, and the program completes via the RISC-V semihosting `ebreak`-triple.

To make the file offsets agree, vsim's RAM region is **narrowed** in hybrid mode to `[0x80000000, 0x80000000+N)` (matching QEMU virt's `VIRT_DRAM`) instead of the default `[0x0, 0x8FFFFFFF]`. See `hybrid_plan.md` Section 9 - touching `simple_memory.hpp` / `platform.hpp` / `mmap_backing.hpp` without re-reading that section will likely re-break the offset contract.

Microarchitectural state (TLB, caches, BPU, store buffer) is **discarded** at handoff; the existing `--kanata-loop-skip N` mechanism warms up before recording.

- `cmake/{HybridPlugin,HybridFixtures,HybridE2E}.cmake` - top-level CTest registrations and `add_custom_target` wrappers around the standalone Makefiles.

## Architecture: how a hybrid run flows

A round-trip exercises three processes sharing a single packed `struct hybrid_state_v1` (defined in `include/hybrid/state_abi.h`, with `static_assert` offsets in `state_abi_check.h`):

1. **QEMU 1** runs with `qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so` loaded via `-plugin`. The plugin matches `csrrw rd,0x7C0,rs1` (mask `0xfff03073`); on `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` it drains GPR/FP/CSR via `qemu_plugin_read_register()`, writes the ABI struct to `outfile=`, and calls `qemu_plugin_request_exit()`.
2. **vsim** starts with `--resume-from-qemu <state.bin>`. Resume is driven **in-process** through the existing JTAG/DTM (`hybrid_vsim/src/platform/jtag_dtm.hpp`): halt -> Abstract Commands write x1..x31 + DPC -> `dret`. No DPI shim, no OpenOCD socket. vsim then runs cycle-accurately until a retire of a pre-scanned `csrw 0x7C0, 1` PC fires `CsrTrigger`, drains state again via DM, writes `--handoff-out`, and exits with `EXIT_HANDOFF_TO_QEMU = 200`.
3. **vsim's main** (on exit-200, when `--qemu-binary` is set) spawns **QEMU 2** with `-gdb -S -semihosting`. `qemu_handback.hpp` connects to the gdbstub, restores GPRs+PC, sends `c`, and the program completes via the RISC-V semihosting `ebreak`-triple.

Memory is **shared, not copied**: when `--shared-mem-path` is given, vsim's `SimpleMemory` is backed by an mmap of `/dev/shm/...` and QEMU is launched with `-object memory-backend-file,share=on,mem-path=...`. To make the file offsets agree, vsim's RAM region is **narrowed** in hybrid mode to `[0x80000000, 0x80000000+N)` (matching QEMU virt's `VIRT_DRAM`) instead of the default `[0x0, 0x8FFFFFFF]`. See `hybrid_plan.md` Section 9 - touching `simple_memory.hpp` / `platform.hpp` / `mmap_backing.hpp` without re-reading that section will likely re-break the offset contract.

Microarchitectural state (TLB, caches, BPU, store buffer) is **discarded** at handoff; the existing `--kanata-loop-skip N` mechanism warms up before recording.

## Critical files (most changes touch these)

Top-level driver assets:

- `include/hybrid/{state_abi.h,state_abi_check.h,insn_match.h,reg_names.h}` - shared wire-ABI; one source of truth for the QEMU plugin and vsim.
- `qemu_plugin/{hybrid_handoff.c,Makefile}` - QEMU TCG plugin. Standalone Make build; `-I../include` resolves to `include/hybrid/`.
- `fixtures/*.S` + `handoff*.ld` + `Makefile` - bare-metal RV64 fixtures using the three-phase template (Phase A in QEMU 1 -> `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` -> Phase B in vsim -> `csrwi 0x7C0, 1` -> Phase C in QEMU 2 -> semihosting `SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED`). Pass/fail signal is the QEMU 2 exit code.
- `tests/{roundtrip_e2e,rt_*}.sh` + `qemu_silent_stub.py` - end-to-end shell drivers that orchestrate QEMU+vsim+QEMU. `REPO_ROOT=$SCRIPT_DIR/..` resolves to this workspace root; defaults look under `build/hybrid_vsim/sim_<cpu>`, `qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so`, `fixtures/<name>.elf`.
- `cmake/HybridE2E.cmake` - top-level CTest registrations only. Plugin and fixtures are driven by their own Makefiles via `build.sh`, not as cmake targets (see "Common commands" above for why).

Vsim-internal integration (stays in the submodule):

- `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/`:
  - `csr_trigger.hpp` - retire-PC watcher; pre-scans the ELF for `csrw 0x7C0, *` PCs.
  - `handoff_controller.hpp` - decodes `csrrw` form, dispatches enter (rs1==0) vs runtime-exit (rs1==1).
  - `state_drain.hpp` / `resume_driver.hpp` - DM Abstract-Command read/write of GPRs+DPC.
  - `qemu_handback.hpp` - spawns QEMU 2; builds `-object memory-backend-file` argv when `shared_mem_path` is set.
  - `gdbstub_client.hpp` / `gdbstub_session.hpp` - in-process gdbstub used to push state into QEMU 2.
  - `resume_from_qemu.hpp`, `state_loader.hpp` - ABI-struct ingest on the vsim side.
  - `exit_codes.hpp` - `EXIT_HANDOFF_TO_QEMU = 200` and friends.
- `hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` - registers vsim-linked hybrid unit tests; reads `${HYBRID_DRIVER_DIR}/include` for the wire-ABI include path.

## Conventions specific to this repo

- **QEMU configure flags are pinned**: `build.sh qemu` runs `../configure --target-list=riscv64-softmmu,riscv64-linux-user --disable-werror --disable-libnfs`. Plugins default to ON in QEMU 9.x (no `--enable-plugins` needed). `--disable-libnfs` works around a host-package mismatch that breaks the configure step; do not remove it without retesting on the build host. `riscv64-linux-user` is bundled with softmmu so the same binary can run user-mode RV64 ELFs.
- **Hybrid is baked into vsim's main.cpp** (not gated by `VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID`). vsim therefore requires `HYBRID_DRIVER_DIR` to be set to a directory containing `include/hybrid/state_abi.h`. The top-level CMake project does this automatically (`add_subdirectory(hybrid_vsim)` forwards `HYBRID_DRIVER_DIR=${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}`). Standalone `cmake -S hybrid_vsim ...` invocations need `-DHYBRID_DRIVER_DIR=/path/to/hybrid_sim` explicitly.
- **Submodules pinned to `hybrid` branch**: never push submodule changes from this superproject without first pushing the corresponding branch in the submodule's own remote.
- **Three-phase fixture template**: every new `rt_*.S` fixture must encode pass/fail into a GPR before `csrwi 0x7C0, 1`, then verify in Phase C and ship the result through semihosting `SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED`. FP/CSR/V state cannot be used as test channels - they are not yet round-tripped (Phase 5 work).
- **Hybrid state ABI is versioned**: `state_abi.h` carries magic `'AHYB'` + version; offsets are `static_assert`-checked in `state_abi_check.h`. Any layout change requires bumping the version and updating both the C plugin and C++ loaders in the same commit.
- **What is intentionally out of scope inside a ROI**: MMIO, lr/sc reservations across handoff, self-modifying code, and `mcycle`/`minstret` continuity. Don't add fallback paths for these without a Phase-5 plan.

lines: +- **vsim builds inside the `vsim-env` container, not on the host**: the host typically lacks Verilator and the apt list in `hybrid_vsim/Dockerfile`. `build.sh vsim` mounts the workspace at `/work` (with `-u $(id -u):$(id -g)` to avoid root-owned outputs) and runs `cmake -S /work -B /work/build` inside the container. The build dir at `build/` is shared with the host so plugin/fixtures (host-built) and ctest (host-run) can see vsim's artifacts.

- **QEMU configure flags are pinned**: `build.sh qemu` runs `../configure --target-list=riscv64-softmmu,riscv64-linux-user --disable-werror --disable-libnfs`. Plugins default to ON in QEMU 9.x (no `--enable-plugins` needed). `--disable-libnfs` works around a host-package mismatch that breaks the configure step; do not remove it without retesting on the build host. `riscv64-linux-user` is bundled with softmmu so the same binary can run user-mode RV64 ELFs.

- **Hybrid is baked into vsim's main.cpp** (not gated by `VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID`). vsim therefore requires `HYBRID_DRIVER_DIR` to be set to a directory containing `include/hybrid/state_abi.h`. The top-level CMake project does this automatically (`add_subdirectory(hybrid_vsim)` forwards `HYBRID_DRIVER_DIR=${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}`). Standalone `cmake -S hybrid_vsim ...` invocations need `-DHYBRID_DRIVER_DIR=/path/to/hybrid_sim` explicitly.
